title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: Thank contributing! guide covers two tracks: Track — Porting SAS template (biostatisticians analysts): adding new plot function sample-data generator. Track B — Package infrastructure (R package developers): dependency changes, CI, testing, CRAN compliance. detailed walkthroughs see Contributing vignette (rendered vignettes/contributing.html pkgdown site).

title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
code: # 1. Install development dependencies install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)  # 2. Load the package without installing devtools::load_all()  # 3. Run all checks (must pass before a PR is merged) devtools::check()  # 4. Run tests only devtools::test()  # 5. Regenerate documentation devtools::document()

what: Track A — Porting a SAS template
title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: SAS template (e.g. tp.np.afib.ivwristm.avrg_curv.binary.sas) want add R equivalent: Create R/-plot.R — one file per plot family. Write bare-ggplot function — accept model-output data frames column-name strings; return unstyled ggplot object. Add sample_my_data() companion — realistic synthetic data mirrors SAS predict / means datasets. Write roxygen docs — include @param, @return, @seealso, @examples, @references (SAS template name), @export. Run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE .Rd files. Add worked example vignettes/plot-functions.qmd. Add row lookup table vignettes/sas-migration-guide.qmd. Add function right section _pkgdown.yml. Write least two tests tests/testthat/test_my_plot.R: one checking return class, one checking sample-data shape. Update NEWS.md current dev version. Open pull request main. See Contributing vignette fully worked example.

title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: changes DESCRIPTION, CI workflows, testing infrastructure, package-level architecture: New dependencies must justified added right field (Imports runtime, Suggests vignettes/tests ). CI runs R CMD CHECK push via GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/). checks must pass. Vignettes built Quarto (VignetteBuilder: quarto). Chunks require file system path network access must #| eval: false. Breaking changes existing function signatures require NEWS.md entry deprecation notice function.

title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: Open issue discuss new port writing code. Tag @ehrlinger review pull requests.

text: R ≥ 4.1 RStudio (recommended) R-capable editor Git Clone repository install dependencies declared DESCRIPTION: devtools::load_all() re-sources R/*.R files makes every exported function available immediately — much faster install.packages() development.
code: # Clone (first time only) # git clone https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR.git  # Install devtools if needed install.packages("devtools")  # Install all Imports + Suggests from DESCRIPTION devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE) # Load the package into the current session without installing devtools::load_all()  # Regenerate NAMESPACE and .Rd files from roxygen comments devtools::document()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Full R CMD CHECK (must pass before merging) devtools::check()  # Build vignettes only devtools::build_vignettes()

what: Track A — Porting a SAS template
title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: track walks adding brand-new plot function porting existing SAS template. use fictional template tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas running example. writing R code, identify: datasets template produce? tp.np.* templates export predict dataset (fitted curve) means dataset (binned patient-level summaries). Export CSV SAS use development data. SAS column names? Document mapping roxygen @description block. example: existing R function closest? Check _pkgdown.yml plot-functions vignette. may need extend existing function rather create new one. Create R/-curve-plot.R (use kebab-case filename). Every plot family lives file — plot functions sample-data companions together. Key rules enforced : Column names strings, passed via x_col =, estimate_col =, etc. Never use enquo() { } — makes column names opaque caller. colours themes applied inside function. caller adds scale_colour_*() hvti_theme() outside. .data[[col]] used tidy evaluation instead bare variable names. requires @importFrom rlang .data. function returns p, print(p) p + theme(...). Add sample_bmi_curve_data() file. generator : Accept n (patient count), time_max / range, seed. Return data frame whose column names match R defaults (time, estimate, lower, upper) — SAS column names. Produce realistic-looking data plausible scale. documentation block must include following: Run devtools::document() editing check parse errors. Add new functions appropriate section _pkgdown.yml. existing section fits, add new one: Open vignettes/plot-functions.qmd add new top-level section “Draft Footnotes” section: vignettes/sas-migration-guide.qmd, add row lookup table top: add corresponding section runnable example # Nonparametric temporal trends family. Create tests/testthat/test_bmi_curve_plot.R: Run devtools::test(filter = "bmi"). Add bullet current dev version top NEWS.md: open pull request main GitHub.
code: # File: R/bmi-curve-plot.R  #' Average BMI Curve Plot #' #' Plots a nonparametric average curve of a binary outcome against BMI #' (or any continuous covariate), with an optional confidence ribbon and #' binned data summary points. Ports #' \code{tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas}. #' #' **SAS column mapping:** #' - `time`     ← `iv_bmi` (BMI on the x-axis) #' - `estimate` ← `mean_curv` (predicted probability) #' - `lower`    ← `cll_p68`  (68 % CI lower) #' - `upper`    ← `clu_p68`  (68 % CI upper) #' #' @param curve_data  Data frame of fitted curve output (one row per x value). #' @param x_col       Name of the x-axis column. Default `"time"`. #' @param estimate_col Name of the predicted value column. Default `"estimate"`. #' @param lower_col   Name of the lower CI column, or `NULL` for no ribbon. #'   Default `NULL`. #' @param upper_col   Name of the upper CI column, or `NULL`. Default `NULL`. #' @param data_points Optional data frame of binned summary points. #'   Must have columns `x_col` and `"value"`. Default `NULL`. #' @param line_width  Width of the curve line. Default `1.0`. #' @param point_size  Size of data summary points. Default `2.5`. #' #' @return A bare [ggplot2::ggplot()] object. #' #' @seealso [nonparametric_curve_plot()], [sample_bmi_curve_data()] #' #' @references SAS template: \code{tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas}. #' #' @examples #' library(ggplot2) #' dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 500) #' bmi_curve_plot(dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper") + #'   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") + #'   scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") + #'   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(18, 45), breaks = seq(20, 45, 5)) + #'   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5), #'                      labels = scales::percent) + #'   labs(x = "BMI (kg/m²)", y = "Prevalence of AF") + #'   hvti_theme("manuscript") #' #' @importFrom ggplot2 ggplot aes geom_line geom_ribbon geom_point #' @importFrom rlang .data #' @export bmi_curve_plot <- function(curve_data,                            x_col        = "time",                            estimate_col = "estimate",                            lower_col    = NULL,                            upper_col    = NULL,                            data_points  = NULL,                            line_width   = 1.0,                            point_size   = 2.5) {    # ----- Input checks -------------------------------------------------------   if (!is.data.frame(curve_data))     stop("`curve_data` must be a data frame.")   for (col in c(x_col, estimate_col)) {     if (!(col %in% names(curve_data)))       stop(sprintf("Column '%s' not found in `curve_data`.", col))   }    # ----- Build base plot ----------------------------------------------------   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(     curve_data,     ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[[estimate_col]])   )    # Optional CI ribbon   if (!is.null(lower_col) && !is.null(upper_col)) {     p <- p + ggplot2::geom_ribbon(       ggplot2::aes(ymin = .data[[lower_col]], ymax = .data[[upper_col]],                    fill = "1"),       alpha = 0.2     )   }    # Main curve   p <- p + ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(colour = "1"),     linewidth = line_width   )    # Optional data summary points   if (!is.null(data_points)) {     p <- p + ggplot2::geom_point(       data = data_points,       ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[["value"]],                    colour = "1", shape = "1"),       size = point_size     )   }    p } #' Sample BMI Curve Data #' #' Simulates the fitted curve output from a nonparametric BMI analysis, #' matching the column layout expected by [bmi_curve_plot()]. #' #' @param n       Number of simulated patients (controls CI width). #'   Default `500`. #' @param bmi_min Lower end of the BMI range. Default `18`. #' @param bmi_max Upper end of the BMI range. Default `50`. #' @param n_points Number of points on the prediction grid. Default `200`. #' @param seed    Random seed. Default `42`. #' #' @return A data frame with columns `time` (BMI grid), `estimate`, #'   `lower`, `upper`. #' #' @seealso [bmi_curve_plot()] #' #' @examples #' dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 300) #' head(dat) #' range(dat$time)     # BMI range #' range(dat$estimate) # probability range, 0-1 #' #' @importFrom stats plogis runif qnorm #' @export sample_bmi_curve_data <- function(n       = 500,                                   bmi_min = 18,                                   bmi_max = 50,                                   n_points = 200,                                   seed    = 42L) {   set.seed(seed)   z    <- stats::qnorm(0.84)   # 68 % CI ≈ 1 SD   bmi  <- seq(bmi_min, bmi_max, length.out = n_points)   eta  <- -2 + 0.05 * (bmi - 30)   # logistic link   est  <- stats::plogis(eta)   se   <- sqrt(est * (1 - est) / (n * 0.02))    data.frame(     time     = bmi,     estimate = est,     lower    = pmax(est - z * se, 0),     upper    = pmin(est + z * se, 1)   ) } - title: "Nonparametric Covariate Curves"   desc: >     Average curves plotted against a continuous covariate (BMI, age, etc.)     rather than against time.   contents:   - bmi_curve_plot   - sample_bmi_curve_data # BMI Curve Plot  `bmi_curve_plot()` plots a fitted nonparametric average curve of a binary outcome against BMI ...   ::: {.cell}  ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 500) bmi_curve_plot(dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper") + ... ``` ::: | `tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas` | np | `bmi_curve_plot()` | [BMI curve](#np-bmi) | library(testthat) library(ggplot2)  test_that("sample_bmi_curve_data returns a data frame with required columns", {   dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   expect_true(is.data.frame(dat))   expect_true(all(c("time", "estimate", "lower", "upper") %in% names(dat))) })  test_that("sample_bmi_curve_data respects n_points", {   dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n_points = 50, seed = 1)   expect_equal(nrow(dat), 50) })  test_that("bmi_curve_plot returns a ggplot", {   dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   p   <- bmi_curve_plot(dat)   expect_s3_class(p, "ggplot") })  test_that("bmi_curve_plot adds ribbon when CI columns are supplied", {   dat    <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   p_ci   <- bmi_curve_plot(dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper")   layers <- sapply(p_ci$layers, function(l) class(l$geom)[1])   expect_true("GeomRibbon" %in% layers) })  test_that("bmi_curve_plot errors on missing column", {   dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 100)   expect_error(bmi_curve_plot(dat, x_col = "no_such_col")) }) * Added `bmi_curve_plot()` and `sample_bmi_curve_data()` — nonparametric   average curve of a binary outcome against a continuous covariate (BMI).   Ports `tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas`. devtools::document()   # regenerate NAMESPACE + .Rd — must complete without errors devtools::test()       # all tests pass devtools::check()      # 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes (ideally)

text: Create R/-curve-plot.R (use kebab-case filename). Every plot family lives file — plot functions sample-data companions together. Key rules enforced : Column names strings, passed via x_col =, estimate_col =, etc. Never use enquo() { } — makes column names opaque caller. colours themes applied inside function. caller adds scale_colour_*() hvti_theme() outside. .data[[col]] used tidy evaluation instead bare variable names. requires @importFrom rlang .data. function returns p, print(p) p + theme(...).
code: # File: R/bmi-curve-plot.R  #' Average BMI Curve Plot #' #' Plots a nonparametric average curve of a binary outcome against BMI #' (or any continuous covariate), with an optional confidence ribbon and #' binned data summary points. Ports #' \code{tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas}. #' #' **SAS column mapping:** #' - `time`     ← `iv_bmi` (BMI on the x-axis) #' - `estimate` ← `mean_curv` (predicted probability) #' - `lower`    ← `cll_p68`  (68 % CI lower) #' - `upper`    ← `clu_p68`  (68 % CI upper) #' #' @param curve_data  Data frame of fitted curve output (one row per x value). #' @param x_col       Name of the x-axis column. Default `"time"`. #' @param estimate_col Name of the predicted value column. Default `"estimate"`. #' @param lower_col   Name of the lower CI column, or `NULL` for no ribbon. #'   Default `NULL`. #' @param upper_col   Name of the upper CI column, or `NULL`. Default `NULL`. #' @param data_points Optional data frame of binned summary points. #'   Must have columns `x_col` and `"value"`. Default `NULL`. #' @param line_width  Width of the curve line. Default `1.0`. #' @param point_size  Size of data summary points. Default `2.5`. #' #' @return A bare [ggplot2::ggplot()] object. #' #' @seealso [nonparametric_curve_plot()], [sample_bmi_curve_data()] #' #' @references SAS template: \code{tp.np.bmi.avrg_curv.binary.sas}. #' #' @examples #' library(ggplot2) #' dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 500) #' bmi_curve_plot(dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper") + #'   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") + #'   scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") + #'   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(18, 45), breaks = seq(20, 45, 5)) + #'   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5), #'                      labels = scales::percent) + #'   labs(x = "BMI (kg/m²)", y = "Prevalence of AF") + #'   hvti_theme("manuscript") #' #' @importFrom ggplot2 ggplot aes geom_line geom_ribbon geom_point #' @importFrom rlang .data #' @export bmi_curve_plot <- function(curve_data,                            x_col        = "time",                            estimate_col = "estimate",                            lower_col    = NULL,                            upper_col    = NULL,                            data_points  = NULL,                            line_width   = 1.0,                            point_size   = 2.5) {    # ----- Input checks -------------------------------------------------------   if (!is.data.frame(curve_data))     stop("`curve_data` must be a data frame.")   for (col in c(x_col, estimate_col)) {     if (!(col %in% names(curve_data)))       stop(sprintf("Column '%s' not found in `curve_data`.", col))   }    # ----- Build base plot ----------------------------------------------------   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(     curve_data,     ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[[estimate_col]])   )    # Optional CI ribbon   if (!is.null(lower_col) && !is.null(upper_col)) {     p <- p + ggplot2::geom_ribbon(       ggplot2::aes(ymin = .data[[lower_col]], ymax = .data[[upper_col]],                    fill = "1"),       alpha = 0.2     )   }    # Main curve   p <- p + ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(colour = "1"),     linewidth = line_width   )    # Optional data summary points   if (!is.null(data_points)) {     p <- p + ggplot2::geom_point(       data = data_points,       ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[["value"]],                    colour = "1", shape = "1"),       size = point_size     )   }    p }

title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: Add sample_bmi_curve_data() file. generator : Accept n (patient count), time_max / range, seed. Return data frame whose column names match R defaults (time, estimate, lower, upper) — SAS column names. Produce realistic-looking data plausible scale.
code: #' Sample BMI Curve Data #' #' Simulates the fitted curve output from a nonparametric BMI analysis, #' matching the column layout expected by [bmi_curve_plot()]. #' #' @param n       Number of simulated patients (controls CI width). #'   Default `500`. #' @param bmi_min Lower end of the BMI range. Default `18`. #' @param bmi_max Upper end of the BMI range. Default `50`. #' @param n_points Number of points on the prediction grid. Default `200`. #' @param seed    Random seed. Default `42`. #' #' @return A data frame with columns `time` (BMI grid), `estimate`, #'   `lower`, `upper`. #' #' @seealso [bmi_curve_plot()] #' #' @examples #' dat <- sample_bmi_curve_data(n = 300) #' head(dat) #' range(dat$time)     # BMI range #' range(dat$estimate) # probability range, 0-1 #' #' @importFrom stats plogis runif qnorm #' @export sample_bmi_curve_data <- function(n       = 500,                                   bmi_min = 18,                                   bmi_max = 50,                                   n_points = 200,                                   seed    = 42L) {   set.seed(seed)   z    <- stats::qnorm(0.84)   # 68 % CI ≈ 1 SD   bmi  <- seq(bmi_min, bmi_max, length.out = n_points)   eta  <- -2 + 0.05 * (bmi - 30)   # logistic link   est  <- stats::plogis(eta)   se   <- sqrt(est * (1 - est) / (n * 0.02))    data.frame(     time     = bmi,     estimate = est,     lower    = pmax(est - z * se, 0),     upper    = pmin(est + z * se, 1)   ) }

what: Step 10: Final checklist before opening a PR
title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: open pull request main GitHub.
code: devtools::document()   # regenerate NAMESPACE + .Rd — must complete without errors devtools::test()       # all tests pass devtools::check()      # 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes (ideally)

title: Contributing to hvtiPlotR
text: NAMESPACE man/*.Rd auto-generated devtools::document() roxygen comments R/*.R. Never edit files hand. Runtime dependency (used inside plot function): add Imports DESCRIPTION declare @importFrom pkg fn roxygen block. Vignette/test : add Suggests. Wrap code uses (requireNamespace("pkg", quietly = TRUE)) { ... } use #| eval: false vignette chunk. Internal helpers (prefixed .) @keywords internal @export — appear NAMESPACE generate .Rd files. Every plot function must: Accept model-output data frames (raw patient data). Map column references string arguments (x_col =, etc.). Use .data[[col]] tidy evaluation inside aes(). Return unstyled ggplot — scale_*(), labs(), theme. call print() invisible(). pattern lets callers layer choices enables + composition grammar documented vignettes/plot-decorators.qmd. Always declare .data roxygen block: source file R/-plot.R corresponding tests/testthat/test_my_plot.R. minimum set tests new plot function: tests/testthat/_snaps/ directory stores snapshot outputs expect_snapshot() tests. update snapshots intentional change: examples @examples blocks run without error. exported functions documentation. NAMESPACE matches actual exports. undefined global variables (lintr / R CMD CHECK NOTE). Vignette chunks marked eval: true run successfully. Vignettes live vignettes/ .qmd files built Quarto (VignetteBuilder: quarto). Rules: code chunks read files, write files, access network must #| eval: false. Use ::() file paths eval-false chunks (hard-coded absolute paths). Use system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR") bundled PPT template — never hard-coded path. adding new vignette, register _pkgdown.yml articles. Update version DESCRIPTION (follow semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Move dev entries NEWS.md new # hvtiPlotR X.Y.Z heading. Run devtools::check() — zero errors zero warnings required. Tag release commit: git tag -vX.Y.Z -m "Release X.Y.Z". Push tag: git push origin vX.Y.Z. pkgdown GitHub Action rebuilds documentation site automatically.
code: hvtiPlotR/ ├── R/                    # Source: one file per plot family ├── man/                  # Auto-generated .Rd files — do not edit manually ├── tests/ │   └── testthat/         # One test_*.R per source file ├── vignettes/            # Quarto (.qmd) vignettes ├── inst/                 # Bundled files (ClevelandClinic.pptx, extdata/) ├── DESCRIPTION           # Package metadata and dependency declarations ├── NAMESPACE             # Auto-generated — do not edit manually ├── _pkgdown.yml          # Website reference and articles layout └── NEWS.md               # User-visible changelog # Check before adding — is it already available in base R or an existing Import? # Keep Imports lean; every new dependency adds installation weight. # Correct — column name is a string; .data masks the data frame ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[[estimate_col]])  # Wrong — bare symbol; fails when x_col is a variable ggplot2::aes(x = x_col, y = estimate_col)  # Wrong — enquo; column name is opaque to the caller ggplot2::aes(x = !!rlang::enquo(x_col)) #' @importFrom rlang .data testthat::snapshot_review()   # review diffs interactively testthat::snapshot_accept()   # accept all pending diffs devtools::test()               # run all tests devtools::test(filter = "bmi") # run tests matching "bmi" devtools::check()              # full R CMD CHECK rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck(          # more detailed output   args = "--no-manual",   error_on = "warning" )

Map column references string arguments (x_col =, etc.). Use .data[[col]] tidy evaluation inside aes(). Return unstyled ggplot — scale_*(), labs(), theme. call print() invisible(). pattern lets callers layer choices enables + composition grammar documented vignettes/plot-decorators.qmd. code: # Correct — column name is a string; .data masks the data frame ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[x_col]], y = .data[[estimate_col]])  # Wrong — bare symbol; fails when x_col is a variable ggplot2::aes(x = x_col, y = estimate_col)  # Wrong — enquo; column name is opaque to the caller ggplot2::aes(x = !!rlang::enquo(x_col)) #' @importFrom rlang .data

code: *_____________________________________________________________________; * C G M F I L E S F O R P O W E R P O I N T S L I D E S *_____________________________________________________________________ * Competing risks , parametric only ; filename gsasfile "&STUDY/graphs/ce.states.ST.cgm "; %plot(goptions gsfmode =replace , device =cgmmppa , ftext=hwcgm001 , end;   axisx order =(0 to 5 by 1), minor=none , value =( height =2.4) , end;   axisy order =(0 to 100 by 20) , minor=none , value =( height =2.4) ,     value =( height =2.4 j=r ' ' '20 ' '40' '60 ' '80 ' '100 '), end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=noinit , width =3, color=gray , end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=nostrk , width =3, color=red , end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=nodeath , width =3, color=blue , end; ); run; # Install from GitHub using the remotes package install.packages("remotes")   # if not already installed remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR") library(haven)  # Read the xport file — variable labels are stored as column attributes dtFilename <- system.file("extdata", "par_cst.xpt", package = "hvtiPlotR") dta <- haven::read_xpt(dtFilename)  # Extract labels into a named character vector for use in labs() calls dta_labels <- sapply(dta, function(col) {   lbl <- attr(col, "label")   if (is.null(lbl) || nchar(trimws(lbl)) == 0) NA_character_ else lbl }) # dta_labels["years"]  →  "Years after operation" dta <- haven::read_sas("path/to/analysis.sas7bdat") proc export data=mean_curv   outfile="&STUDY/graphs/mean_curv.csv"   dbms=csv replace; run; dta <- read.csv(here::here("graphs", "mean_curv.csv")) par_file  <- system.file("extdata", "par_cst.xpt",  package = "hvtiPlotR") npar_file <- system.file("extdata", "npar_cst.xpt", package = "hvtiPlotR")  parametric    <- haven::read_xpt(par_file) nonparametric <- haven::read_xpt(npar_file) # load required libraries library("ggplot2") # Plotting environment if (requireNamespace("hvtiPlotR", quietly = TRUE)) {   library("hvtiPlotR") # Use installed package when available } else {   pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE, helpers = FALSE, quiet = TRUE) }  # Load the example datasets data(parametric, package = "hvtiPlotR") data(nonparametric, package = "hvtiPlotR")  # Set a default hvtiPlotR plotting theme theme_set(hvtiPlotR::hvti_theme("manuscript")) ## To reproduce the plot.sas function, line by line. ###------------- ## There are SAS options we will not use here. # # %plot(goptions gsfmode=replace, device=pscolor, gaccess=gsasfile end; ccf_plot <- ggplot() ###------------- ## Labels are a single command, scales control the axis # # labelx l="Years After Randomization", end; # labely l="Percent in Each Category (ST)", end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   labs(x = "Years After Randomization", y = "Percent in Each Category (ST)") ###------------- ## Labels are a single command, scales control the axis # # axisx order=(0 to 5 by 1), minor=none, end; # axisy order=(0 to 100 by 10), minor=none, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) ###------------- ## /******NON-PARAMETRIC: SYMBOLS AND CONFIDENCE BARS *******/ ## ## Each tuple statement corresponds to one or more geom_ statements # tuple set=green, symbol=dot, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sginit, cll=stlinit, clu=stuinit, color=black, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(data = nonparametric, aes(x = iv_state, y = sginit))  show(ccf_plot) # tuple set=green, symbol=circle, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sgdead1, cll=stldead1, clu=studead1, color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, y = sgdead1),     color = "blue",     shape = 1   ) +   geom_errorbar(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, ymin = stldead1, ymax = studead1),     color = "blue",     width = .1   ) # tuple set=green, symbol=square, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sgstrk1, cll=stlstrk1, clu=stustrk1, color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, y = sgstrk1),     color = "blue",     shape = 0   ) +   geom_errorbar(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, ymin = stlstrk1, ymax = stustrk1),     color = "blue",     width = .1   ) show(ccf_plot) Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: document, assume data analysis completed SAS. first step creating figures R get data SAS. two common approaches. Use haven package (included hvtiPlotR’s Suggests) read SAS xport (.xpt) files. preserves variable labels label attribute column, accessible attr(dta$varname, "label"). haven also reads SAS7BDAT files directly haven::read_sas(), convenient route SAS server accessible: Export summary dataset SAS using PROC EXPORT: read R: simplest approach works well pre-computed summary datasets expected hvtiPlotR plot functions (e.g., nonparametric_curve_plot(), hazard_plot()). hvtiPlotR ships two example xport files testing:
code: library(haven)  # Read the xport file — variable labels are stored as column attributes dtFilename <- system.file("extdata", "par_cst.xpt", package = "hvtiPlotR") dta <- haven::read_xpt(dtFilename)  # Extract labels into a named character vector for use in labs() calls dta_labels <- sapply(dta, function(col) {   lbl <- attr(col, "label")   if (is.null(lbl) || nchar(trimws(lbl)) == 0) NA_character_ else lbl }) # dta_labels["years"]  →  "Years after operation" dta <- haven::read_sas("path/to/analysis.sas7bdat") proc export data=mean_curv   outfile="&STUDY/graphs/mean_curv.csv"   dbms=csv replace; run; dta <- read.csv(here::here("graphs", "mean_curv.csv")) par_file  <- system.file("extdata", "par_cst.xpt",  package = "hvtiPlotR") npar_file <- system.file("extdata", "npar_cst.xpt", package = "hvtiPlotR")  parametric    <- haven::read_xpt(par_file) nonparametric <- haven::read_xpt(npar_file)

Listing 2 turns oﬀ lines points shown (linepe=0, linecl=0,). also handles error bars (ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1), discuss Section 3.6. last line tells macro point placement using vector x y coordinates. Points displayed paired (x, y) error bars specified matching y values upper (clu) lower (cll) error bar limits (x=iv_state, y=sginit, cll=stlinit, clu=stuinit). geom_ set functions ggplot2 functional equivalent tuple statement. diﬀerence user specifies graphical element desired using separate function calls. points plotting using geom_point function, lines generated geom_line (Section 3.7) error bars generated geom_errorbar function (Section 3.6). functions can take data argument well large set decorator arguments (.e. color, size, shape, linetype, . . . ). aesthetic function (aes()) call used describe point within geom_ function using variable names defined data set. following code chunk demonstrates plotting iv_state variable x-axis sginit variable along y-axis. variables defined nonparametric data set loaded setup code chunk Section 3.  aes() mechanism powerful way communicate data level assignment geom_ functions. want stratify dataset variable, can specify within aes() function call using = argument. points, often want stratifying either diﬀerent color= shape= stratified data. can use scale_color_ functions (See Section 3.10) scale_shape_ functions (See Section 3.9) control assigned stratifying variable. added data ggplot object, can display figure shown Figure 4. now figure manipulated sequentially adding function calls ccf_plot object. display figure can either use show() function, simply call object name command line. Note used default shape, size color figure. can manipulated adding arguments geom_ functions, outside aes() function, demonstrate following sections.

text: created figure, formatted desired (using hvtiPlotR theme), need save figure format can easily imported publications. Use ggsave() write composed figure disk. Dimensions width = 11, height = 8.5 (US Letter landscape) suit manuscript figures. full treatment saving PDF, poster, PowerPoint formats see Decorating Saving hvtiPlotR Plots. Use save_ppt() insert ggplot editable vector graphic PowerPoint file. Apply hvti_theme("dark_ppt") hvti_theme("light_ppt") saving.
code: p_final <- ccf_plot +   labs(     x       = "Years After Randomization",     y       = "Percent in Each Category (ST)",     caption = getwd()   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/manuscript.pdf",   plot     = p_final,   width    = 11,   height   = 8.5 ) p_ppt <- ccf_pptPlot + hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = p_ppt,   template     = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),   powerpoint   = here::here("graphs", "presentation.pptx"),   slide_titles = "Competing Risks" )

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: standards followed CORR (Cardiovascular Outcomes Registries Research) statistics group within Heart & Vascular Institute. Many encoded directly hvtiPlotR themes accidentally violate ; rest conventions keep mind composing figures. Use hvti_theme("manuscript") — black text, white background, decorative fill. Never use coloured backgrounds figures destined journals. chart titles — figure caption manuscript text carries title. add labs(title = ...) layer. Axis labels mandatory — always supply labs(x = ..., y = ...). Units belong axis label, tick labels (e.g. "Years operation", "0 yr, 5 yr, 10 yr"). Percent axes — format y-axis tick labels "0%", "20%", …, "100%" using scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")). write % symbol inside axis title. minor grid lines — major grid lines optional light grey used. Minor grid lines never used. Confidence intervals ribbons error bars — always show CIs available. Use alpha = 0.2 ribbons obscure curve. Save US Letter landscape — ggsave(width = 11, height = 8.5). journals accept dimension; adjust journal specifies otherwise. Identify file — include labs(caption = getwd()) call make_footnote() analysis track script produced figure. Remove final submission. Use hvti_theme("dark_ppt") hvti_theme("light_ppt") — dark theme default Cleveland Clinic presentation templates. Match theme slide background colour. points parametric curves — presentation figures show lines . Points reserved nonparametric data summaries. Larger line widths — use linewidth = 1.5 higher lines clearly visible projected screen. SAS equivalent width=3. Fewer axis ticks — five six ticks per axis maximum slides. Use scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) control spacing. captions — slide titles carry figure description. add labs(caption = ...) presentation figures. Export editable vector — use save_ppt() rather ggsave() lines, shapes, text remain selectable PowerPoint last-minute edits. Multi-group figures — use scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") five groups. groups, select palette manually use RColorBrewer::brewer.pal() inspect options. Single-group survival/hazard figures — "steelblue" manuscript, "white" dark PPT. Avoid red/green combinations — approximately 8% men red–green colour blindness. Use shape (scale_shape_manual()) linetype (scale_linetype_manual()) addition colour figures remain readable greyscale print. Fills vs colours — scale_colour_*() controls lines points; scale_fill_*() controls ribbons bars. must set consistently legend shown. decorative 3-D effects, drop shadows, gradients. background images watermarks. six groups per panel — split separate figures needed. axis tick marks top right-hand sides. connecting lines non-adjacent data points unless relationship continuous (e.g., survival curve).

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: Use hvti_theme("manuscript") — black text, white background, decorative fill. Never use coloured backgrounds figures destined journals. chart titles — figure caption manuscript text carries title. add labs(title = ...) layer. Axis labels mandatory — always supply labs(x = ..., y = ...). Units belong axis label, tick labels (e.g. "Years operation", "0 yr, 5 yr, 10 yr"). Percent axes — format y-axis tick labels "0%", "20%", …, "100%" using scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")). write % symbol inside axis title. minor grid lines — major grid lines optional light grey used. Minor grid lines never used. Confidence intervals ribbons error bars — always show CIs available. Use alpha = 0.2 ribbons obscure curve. Save US Letter landscape — ggsave(width = 11, height = 8.5). journals accept dimension; adjust journal specifies otherwise. Identify file — include labs(caption = getwd()) call make_footnote() analysis track script produced figure. Remove final submission.

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: Use hvti_theme("dark_ppt") hvti_theme("light_ppt") — dark theme default Cleveland Clinic presentation templates. Match theme slide background colour. points parametric curves — presentation figures show lines . Points reserved nonparametric data summaries. Larger line widths — use linewidth = 1.5 higher lines clearly visible projected screen. SAS equivalent width=3. Fewer axis ticks — five six ticks per axis maximum slides. Use scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) control spacing. captions — slide titles carry figure description. add labs(caption = ...) presentation figures. Export editable vector — use save_ppt() rather ggsave() lines, shapes, text remain selectable PowerPoint last-minute edits.

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: Multi-group figures — use scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") five groups. groups, select palette manually use RColorBrewer::brewer.pal() inspect options. Single-group survival/hazard figures — "steelblue" manuscript, "white" dark PPT. Avoid red/green combinations — approximately 8% men red–green colour blindness. Use shape (scale_shape_manual()) linetype (scale_linetype_manual()) addition colour figures remain readable greyscale print. Fills vs colours — scale_colour_*() controls lines points; scale_fill_*() controls ribbons bars. must set consistently legend shown.

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: decorative 3-D effects, drop shadows, gradients. background images watermarks. six groups per panel — split separate figures needed. axis tick marks top right-hand sides. connecting lines non-adjacent data points unless relationship continuous (e.g., survival curve).

title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R

text: John Ehrlinger Heart, Vascular Thoracic Institute Cleveland Clinic 9500 Euclid Ave Cleveland, Ohio 44195 E-mail: ehrlinj@ccf.org URL: http://www.lerner.ccf.org/qhs/people/ehrlinj/ URL: https://github.com/ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: Every hvtiPlotR plot function returns bare ggplot object — colour scales, axis labels, theme applied. Decoration added chaining layers +: vignette demonstrates decorator turn, using trends_plot() survival_curve() representative base plots.
code: bare_plot() +   scale_colour_*() +   # data colours   scale_fill_*()   +   # fill colours   labs()           +   # axis labels, title, caption   annotate()       +   # text/arrows placed on the panel   coord_cartesian() +  # viewport cropping   hvti_theme()         # non-data formatting # Trends data — multi-group continuous outcome over time dta_trends <- sample_trends_data(n = 600, seed = 42) p_base <- trends_plot(dta_trends)  # KM data — survival curve dta_km  <- sample_survival_data(n = 500, seed = 42) km      <- survival_curve(dta_km, alpha = 0.8)

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: hvtiPlotR package provides four themes via hvti_theme(style). style argument selects output target.
code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("poster") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("light_ppt") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt") +   theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "navy", colour = "navy"))

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: scale_colour_* controls line point colours; scale_fill_* controls filled areas (ribbons, bars). share name (legend title) guide (legend display) arguments. Use scale_colour_manual() assigning specific brand convention colours known levels.  scale_colour_brewer() applies ColorBrewer palette — safe, perceptually uniform, print-friendly. Use palette = "Set1" categorical data, "RdYlGn" diverging, "Blues" sequential.  Pass guide = "none" scale remove legend entry. Use colour self-evident axis labels annotations.
code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2", guide = "none") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     guide = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: Use scale_colour_manual() assigning specific brand convention colours known levels.
code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: scale_colour_brewer() applies ColorBrewer palette — safe, perceptually uniform, print-friendly. Use palette = "Set1" categorical data, "RdYlGn" diverging, "Blues" sequential.
code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Dark2", guide = "none") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     guide = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: labs() sets axis, legend, title, subtitle, caption text. Set axis labels rather inside plot function can overridden per project.  annotate() places text, segments, rectangles fixed data coordinates. Use sample size callouts, phase labels, directional arrows.  coord_cartesian() crops viewport without dropping data, preserving LOESS fits computed full range.
code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     title   = "Overall Survival",     x       = "Years after Operation",     y       = "Freedom from Death (%)",     caption = "Logit CI, \u03b1 = 0.6827 (1 SD)"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   annotate("text",    x = 1,  y = 5,            label = paste0("n = ", nrow(dta_km)),            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("segment", x = 10, xend = 10, y = 30, yend = 50,            arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), colour = "grey40") +   annotate("text",    x = 10.3, y = 40,            label = "Median survival", hjust = 0, size = 3, colour = "grey40") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1995, 2020), ylim = c(20, 70)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: labs() sets axis, legend, title, subtitle, caption text. Set axis labels rather inside plot function can overridden per project.
code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     title   = "Overall Survival",     x       = "Years after Operation",     y       = "Freedom from Death (%)",     caption = "Logit CI, \u03b1 = 0.6827 (1 SD)"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   annotate("text",    x = 1,  y = 5,            label = paste0("n = ", nrow(dta_km)),            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("segment", x = 10, xend = 10, y = 30, yend = 50,            arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), colour = "grey40") +   annotate("text",    x = 10.3, y = 40,            label = "Median survival", hjust = 0, size = 3, colour = "grey40") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: coord_cartesian() crops viewport without dropping data, preserving LOESS fits computed full range.
code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1995, 2020), ylim = c(20, 70)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: Use ggsave() width = 11, height = 8.5 (US Letter landscape) manuscript figures. Assign fully composed plot variable first object displayed session written disk. Poster figures typically larger use hvti_theme("poster"). Adjust dimensions match poster panel size. save_ppt() inserts ggplot objects PowerPoint file editable DrawingML vector graphics via officer rvg packages — shapes, lines, text remain selectable PowerPoint export. Key arguments: Apply hvti_theme("dark_ppt") hvti_theme("light_ppt") saving match slide background. Pass named list plots matching vector titles produce one slide per plot single call. generating multiple plots loop, gridExtra::marrangeGrob() arranges grid ggsave() writes page.
code: p_ms <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/trends_manuscript.pdf",   plot     = p_ms,   width    = 11,   height   = 8.5 ) p_poster <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +   hvti_theme("poster")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/trends_poster.pdf",   plot     = p_poster,   width    = 14,   height   = 10 ) template <- system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR")  p_ppt <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = p_ppt,   template     = template,   powerpoint   = here::here("graphs", "trends_slides.pptx"),   slide_titles = "Temporal Trends by Group" ) dta_km2 <- sample_survival_data(n = 400, seed = 99) km2     <- survival_curve(dta_km2)  p_km_ppt <- km2 +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "white"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "white"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = list(trends = p_ppt, survival = p_km_ppt),   template     = template,   powerpoint   = here::here("graphs", "multi_slide_deck.pptx"),   slide_titles = c("Temporal Trends by Group", "Overall Survival") ) # Build a list of plots (e.g. from an eda_plot() lapply loop) plot_list <- lapply(   c("ef", "lv_mass", "peak_grad"),   function(yv) {     dta_eda <- sample_eda_data()     eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "op_years", y_col = yv,              y_label = yv) +       scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +       labs(x = "Years") +       hvti_theme("manuscript")   } )  per_page <- 9L for (pg in seq(1, length(plot_list), by = per_page)) {   idx  <- seq(pg, min(pg + per_page - 1L, length(plot_list)))   grob <- gridExtra::marrangeGrob(plot_list[idx], nrow = 3, ncol = 3)   ggsave(     filename = sprintf(here::here("graphs", "eda_page%02d.pdf"),                        ceiling(pg / per_page)),     plot     = grob,     width    = 14,     height   = 14   ) }

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: Use ggsave() width = 11, height = 8.5 (US Letter landscape) manuscript figures. Assign fully composed plot variable first object displayed session written disk.
code: p_ms <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/trends_manuscript.pdf",   plot     = p_ms,   width    = 11,   height   = 8.5 )

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: save_ppt() inserts ggplot objects PowerPoint file editable DrawingML vector graphics via officer rvg packages — shapes, lines, text remain selectable PowerPoint export. Key arguments: Apply hvti_theme("dark_ppt") hvti_theme("light_ppt") saving match slide background. Pass named list plots matching vector titles produce one slide per plot single call.
code: template <- system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR")  p_ppt <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = p_ppt,   template     = template,   powerpoint   = here::here("graphs", "trends_slides.pptx"),   slide_titles = "Temporal Trends by Group" ) dta_km2 <- sample_survival_data(n = 400, seed = 99) km2     <- survival_curve(dta_km2)  p_km_ppt <- km2 +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "white"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "white"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = list(trends = p_ppt, survival = p_km_ppt),   template     = template,   powerpoint   = here::here("graphs", "multi_slide_deck.pptx"),   slide_titles = c("Temporal Trends by Group", "Overall Survival") )

text: generating multiple plots loop, gridExtra::marrangeGrob() arranges grid ggsave() writes page.
code: # Build a list of plots (e.g. from an eda_plot() lapply loop) plot_list <- lapply(   c("ef", "lv_mass", "peak_grad"),   function(yv) {     dta_eda <- sample_eda_data()     eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "op_years", y_col = yv,              y_label = yv) +       scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +       labs(x = "Years") +       hvti_theme("manuscript")   } )  per_page <- 9L for (pg in seq(1, length(plot_list), by = per_page)) {   idx  <- seq(pg, min(pg + per_page - 1L, length(plot_list)))   grob <- gridExtra::marrangeGrob(plot_list[idx], nrow = 3, ncol = 3)   ggsave(     filename = sprintf(here::here("graphs", "eda_page%02d.pdf"),                        ceiling(pg / per_page)),     plot     = grob,     width    = 14,     height   = 14   ) }

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: ggplot2 places legend outside panel default. publication figures often cleaner place inside panel suppress entirely. Pass fractional coordinates c(x, y) legend.position inside theme(). c(0, 0) bottom-left corner; c(1, 1) top-right.    guide = "none" every scale_*() call removes legend entries. preferred theme(legend.position = "none") aesthetics legends others .
code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     legend.position  = c(0.15, 0.2),        # bottom-left of panel     legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey80",                                      linewidth = 0.3)   ) p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     legend.position = "bottom",             # "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom"     legend.direction = "horizontal"   ) km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),     legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm")   )

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: hvti_theme() sets complete non-data formatting baseline. Layer additional theme() calls adjust individual elements without touching rest.   Useful time-point labels long category names.  Titles stripped base themes (rarely used journal figures), can added back:  Add breathing room around panel, example figure placed directly poster without surrounding text frame.
code: p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),   # tick labels     axis.title = element_text(size = 12)    # axis titles   ) p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     panel.grid.minor = element_blank()   ) p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)   ) km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     title    = "Overall Survival",     subtitle = paste0("n = ", nrow(dta_km), " patients"),     x        = "Years after Operation",     y        = "Freedom from Death (%)"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0),     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, colour = "grey40", hjust = 0)   ) p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("poster") +   theme(     plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 20, b = 10, l = 20, unit = "pt")   )

title: Decorating and Saving hvtiPlotR Plots
text: patchwork package composes multiple ggplot objects single figure. Use place two related plots side side, stack main plot companion table risk panel.  | places plots side side; / stacks vertically.  common pattern survival curves pair plot numbers--risk panel. survival_curve() returns risk table data frame via attr(result, "risk_table") — columns strata, report_time, n.risk. Build ggplot text panel , stack /.  plot_annotation() adds shared title tags (, B, C…) across panels.  Assign composed object variable pass ggsave(). PowerPoint, save panel individually save_ppt() (patchwork composites editable DrawingML objects).
code: library(patchwork)  p_ms <- p_base +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Group I" = 15, "Group II" = 19,                "Group III" = 17, "Group IV" = 18),     name = "Group"   ) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Outcome") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  p_km_ms <- km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  p_ms | p_km_ms (p_ms | p_km_ms) +   plot_layout(widths = c(2, 1))   # left panel twice as wide as right risk_df <- attr(km, "risk_table")  rt_panel <- ggplot(risk_df,                    aes(x = report_time, y = factor(strata),                        label = n.risk)) +   geom_text(size = 3) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = NULL) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     axis.line  = element_blank(),     axis.ticks = element_blank()   )  p_km_ms / rt_panel +   plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1)) (p_ms | p_km_ms) +   plot_annotation(     title = "Figure 1. Outcomes after cardiac surgery",     tag_levels = "A"   ) &   theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")) combined <- (p_ms | p_km_ms) +   plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &   theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))  ggsave(   filename = here::here("graphs", "fig1_combined.pdf"),   plot     = combined,   width    = 14,   height   = 7 )

code: risk_df <- attr(km, "risk_table")  rt_panel <- ggplot(risk_df,                    aes(x = report_time, y = factor(strata),                        label = n.risk)) +   geom_text(size = 3) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = NULL) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(     axis.line  = element_blank(),     axis.ticks = element_blank()   )  p_km_ms / rt_panel +   plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1))

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: table maps hvtiPlotR function original SAS R templates ports. Functions marked — direct predecessor designed specifically package. functions worked examples sections .

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: common figure propensity-matched analyses mirrored histogram, displays propensity score distributions two treatment groups matching. hvtiPlotR package provides mirror_histogram() function generate figure. function accepts data frame columns propensity score, group indicator, match indicator. sample_mirror_histogram_data() function generates example data suitable testing. function supports two display modes selected arguments supplied: Binary-match mode (match_col): upper bars show observations matching; overlaid bars show matched subset. reproduces tp.lp.mirror-histogram_SAVR-TF-TAVR.R. Weighted IPTW mode (weight_col): upper bars show raw counts; overlaid bars show per-bin IPTW weight sums. reproduces tp.lp.mirror_histo_before_after_wt.R.  lighter bars show full (pre-match) distribution group; darker overlaid bars show matched subset. Upper panel = first group label; lower panel = second group label. function returns diagnostics group counts standardized mean differences (SMD) matching:  Weighted diagnostics include effective N weighted SMD:
code: mirror_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 2000, separation = 1.5)  mhist <- mirror_histogram(   data          = mirror_dta,   score_col     = "prob_t",   group_col     = "tavr",   match_col     = "match",   group_levels  = c(0, 1),   group_labels  = c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR"),   matched_value = 1,   score_multiplier = 100,   binwidth      = 5,   alpha         = 0.8 )  mhist +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0  = "white",  matched_g0 = "green1",       before_g1  = "white",  matched_g1 = "green4"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)   ) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 20, y =  100, label = "SAVR",    size = 7) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 20, y = -100, label = "TF-TAVR", size = 7) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "Number of Patients") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$smd_before [1] 1.563175 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$smd_matched [1] 0.02714868 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$group_counts_before 0    1 2000 2000 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$group_counts_matched 0   1 919 919 wt_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(   n = 2000, separation = 1.5, add_weights = TRUE )  mhist_wt <- mirror_histogram(   data         = wt_dta,   score_col    = "prob_t",   group_col    = "tavr",   group_levels = c(0, 1),   group_labels = c("Limited", "Extended"),   weight_col   = "mt_wt",   score_multiplier = 100,   binwidth     = 5,   alpha        = 0.8 )  mhist_wt +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0   = "white", weighted_g0 = "blue",       before_g1   = "white", weighted_g1 = "red"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)   ) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 30, y =  150, label = "Limited",  color = "blue", size = 5) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 30, y = -70,  label = "Extended", color = "red",  size = 5) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "#") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") attr(mhist_wt, "diagnostics")$smd_weighted [1] 0.5434922 attr(mhist_wt, "diagnostics")$effective_n_by_group 0    1 2000 2000

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: lighter bars show full (pre-match) distribution group; darker overlaid bars show matched subset. Upper panel = first group label; lower panel = second group label. function returns diagnostics group counts standardized mean differences (SMD) matching:
code: mirror_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 2000, separation = 1.5)  mhist <- mirror_histogram(   data          = mirror_dta,   score_col     = "prob_t",   group_col     = "tavr",   match_col     = "match",   group_levels  = c(0, 1),   group_labels  = c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR"),   matched_value = 1,   score_multiplier = 100,   binwidth      = 5,   alpha         = 0.8 )  mhist +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0  = "white",  matched_g0 = "green1",       before_g1  = "white",  matched_g1 = "green4"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)   ) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 20, y =  100, label = "SAVR",    size = 7) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 20, y = -100, label = "TF-TAVR", size = 7) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "Number of Patients") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$smd_before [1] 1.563175 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$smd_matched [1] 0.02714868 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$group_counts_before 0    1 2000 2000 attr(mhist, "diagnostics")$group_counts_matched 0   1 919 919

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: Weighted diagnostics include effective N weighted SMD:
code: wt_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(   n = 2000, separation = 1.5, add_weights = TRUE )  mhist_wt <- mirror_histogram(   data         = wt_dta,   score_col    = "prob_t",   group_col    = "tavr",   group_levels = c(0, 1),   group_labels = c("Limited", "Extended"),   weight_col   = "mt_wt",   score_multiplier = 100,   binwidth     = 5,   alpha        = 0.8 )  mhist_wt +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0   = "white", weighted_g0 = "blue",       before_g1   = "white", weighted_g1 = "red"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)   ) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 30, y =  150, label = "Limited",  color = "blue", size = 5) +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 30, y = -70,  label = "Extended", color = "red",  size = 5) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "#") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") attr(mhist_wt, "diagnostics")$smd_weighted [1] 0.5434922 attr(mhist_wt, "diagnostics")$effective_n_by_group 0    1 2000 2000

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: common exploratory figure stacked histogram, shows composition numeric variable changes time across another grouping dimension. hvtiPlotR package provides stacked_histogram() function generate figure. function returns bare ggplot object — colour scales, axis labels, theme applied — caller can add freely usual + operator. sample_stacked_histogram_data() function generates reproducible example dataset year category columns. default position = "stack" shows raw counts within bin, equivalent plot.sas frequency histogram.  Setting position = "fill" rescales bin bars sum 1, making easy compare relative composition across years.
code: # Generate sample data hist_dta <- sample_stacked_histogram_data(n_years = 20, start_year = 2000,                                            n_categories = 3) head(hist_dta) year category 1 2000        1 2 2000        1 3 2000        2 4 2000        2 5 2000        2 6 2000        1 # Build the bare plot p_count <- stacked_histogram(hist_dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category")  # Layer on colour scales, labels, and a theme p_count +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") # Build the proportional variant p_fill <- stacked_histogram(hist_dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category",                              position = "fill")  # Use manual colours and custom legend labels p_fill +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     labels = c("1" = "Group A", "2" = "Group B", "3" = "Group C"),     name   = "Category"   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_final <- p_fill +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     name   = "Category"   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/stacked_histogram.pdf",   plot     = p_final,   width    = 11,   height   = 8 )

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: default position = "stack" shows raw counts within bin, equivalent plot.sas frequency histogram.
code: # Build the bare plot p_count <- stacked_histogram(hist_dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category")  # Layer on colour scales, labels, and a theme p_count +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: goodness--follow-plot standard quality-control figure longitudinal outcome analyses. displays patient point operation date (x-axis) follow-duration (y-axis), short vertical tick point. dashed diagonal line marks maximum potential follow-given study start, study end, follow-closing date. hvtiPlotR package provides goodness_followup() build figure. function returns bare ggplot object colour, shape, axis, label scales applied — added caller standard ggplot2 modifiers. Scale, label, annotation layers composed usual + operator. scale_color_manual() scale_shape_manual() map binary alive/dead state colours point shapes. annotate() places group-identifying text directly panel. diagonal dashed line represents maximum potential follow-. Points sitting line indicate patients longer follow-expected study window, typically due passive surveillance supplementing active cross-sectional follow-. dataset includes non-fatal competing event (e.g. relapse, reoperation), pass event_col, event_time_col, optionally death_for_event_col generate second panel alongside death panel.  death panel (goodness_followup()) event panel (goodness_event_plot()) share diagonal reference line can saved individually ggsave().
code: gfup_dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data(n = 300, seed = 42) head(gfup_dta) iv_opyrs iv_dead  dead iv_event ev_event deads 1  29.5694  2.0261 FALSE   2.0261    FALSE FALSE 2   6.4834  6.9027  TRUE   4.0813     TRUE  TRUE 3  14.4342  5.4468  TRUE   5.4468    FALSE  TRUE 4  25.4324  6.1630 FALSE   0.6137     TRUE FALSE 5   3.4251 13.1369  TRUE   6.9232     TRUE  TRUE 6  24.1620  7.4334 FALSE   7.4334    FALSE FALSE gfup <- goodness_followup(   data        = gfup_dta,   origin_year = 1990,   study_start = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end   = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date  = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   alpha       = 0.8 )  # Bare plot — no scales or labels yet gfup$death_plot NULL library(RColorBrewer)  gfup$death_plot +   # Colour alive = blue, dead = red (Set1 palette positions 2 and 1)   scale_color_manual(     values   = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[c(2, 1)],     labels   = c("Alive", "Dead"),     na.value = "black",     drop     = FALSE   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c(1, 4),     labels = c("Alive", "Dead")   ) +   # Axis tick placement   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2020, 3)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 33,   3)) +   # Clip the panel to the study window   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 33), xlim = c(1990, 2020)) +   # Axis and legend labels   labs(     x     = "Operation Date",     y     = "Follow-up (years)",     color = "Status",     shape = "Status"   ) +   # Annotate directly on the panel   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 31, label = "Alive at close",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 28, label = "Deceased",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[1]) +   theme(legend.position = "none") NULL gfup_final <- gfup$death_plot +   scale_color_manual(     values   = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[c(2, 1)],     labels   = c("Alive", "Dead"),     na.value = "black",     drop     = FALSE   ) +   scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 4), labels = c("Alive", "Dead")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2020, 3)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 33, 3)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 33), xlim = c(1990, 2020)) +   labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)",        color = "Status", shape = "Status") +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 31, label = "Alive at close",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 28, label = "Deceased",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[1]) +   theme(legend.position = "none") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/dp_goodness-of-followup.pdf",   plot     = gfup_final,   height   = 6,   width    = 6 ) gfup_event_dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data(n = 300, seed = 42) goodness_event_plot(   gfup_event_dta,   event_col           = "ev_event",   event_time_col      = "iv_event",   death_for_event_col = "deads",   event_levels        = c("No event", "Relapse", "Death"),   origin_year         = 1990,   study_start         = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end           = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date          = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   alpha               = 0.8 ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("No event" = "blue", "Relapse" = "green3", "Death" = "red"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("No event" = 1L, "Relapse" = 2L, "Death" = 4L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2020, 3)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 33, 3)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 33), xlim = c(1990, 2020)) +   labs(     x = "Operation Date",     y = "Follow-up (years)",     color = "Event", shape = "Event"   ) +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 31,            label = "Systematic follow-up", hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.85, 0.15)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: covariate balance plot standard quality-control figure propensity score matching IPTW analyses. covariate occupies labelled row; points show standardized mean difference (SMD) comparison group (e.g. matching). solid vertical line marks zero balance; dotted vertical lines mark imbalance threshold (default ±10%). hvtiPlotR package provides covariate_balance() build figure, superseding tp.lp.propen.cov_balance.R. returns bare ggplot object — colour, shape, axis labels, theme applied — styling added usual + operator. Input data must long format: one row per covariate × group combination columns covariate name, group label, numeric SMD value. source dataset arrives wide format (one column per time-point, SAS export ), reshape first:   annotate() places explanatory text directly panel indicate direction imbalance favours group. example uses exact labels, x-scale, annotation positions, legend placement tp.lp.propen.cov_balance.R (SAVR vs. TF-TAVR study).  Pass var_levels control bottom--top display order covariates.
code: # Simulate a wide-format export (e.g. from SAS or a summary table) dta_wide <- data.frame(   variable      = c("Age", "Female sex", "Hypertension", "Diabetes", "COPD"),   `Before match` = c(22.1, -15.3,  18.7, -9.4,  11.2),   `After match`  = c( 3.5,   2.1,  -1.8,  4.0,  -2.3),   check.names = FALSE )  dta_long <- reshape(   dta_wide,   direction = "long",   varying   = c("Before match", "After match"),   v.names   = "std_diff",   timevar   = "group",   times     = c("Before match", "After match"),   idvar     = "variable" ) head(dta_long) variable        group std_diff Age.Before match                   Age Before match     22.1 Female sex.Before match     Female sex Before match    -15.3 Hypertension.Before match Hypertension Before match     18.7 Diabetes.Before match         Diabetes Before match     -9.4 COPD.Before match                 COPD Before match     11.2 Age.After match                    Age  After match      3.5 dta_cb <- sample_covariate_balance_data(n_vars = 12) head(dta_cb) variable        group std_diff 1               Age Before match      9.8 2        Female sex Before match     25.4 3      Hypertension Before match    -14.7 4 Diabetes mellitus Before match     -8.9 5              COPD Before match     -3.9 6        Creatinine Before match     26.5 covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(-45, 35),     breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)   ) +   labs(     x = "Standardized difference (%)",     y = ""   ) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.95)) n_vars <- length(unique(dta_cb$variable))  covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(-40, 30),     breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)   ) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference: SAVR - TF:TAVR (%)", y = "") +   annotate("text", x = -30, y = 0,        label = "More likely TF-TAVR", size = 4.5) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = n_vars,   label = "More likely SAVR",    size = 4.5) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.935)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") covariate_balance(   dta_cb,   var_levels = rev(unique(dta_cb$variable)),   alpha      = 0.8 ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference (%)", y = "") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") n_vars   <- length(unique(dta_cb$variable)) cb_final <- covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-40, 30), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference: SAVR - TF:TAVR (%)", y = "") +   annotate("text", x = -30, y = 0,      label = "More likely TF-TAVR", size = 4.5) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = n_vars, label = "More likely SAVR",    size = 4.5) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.935)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = here::here("graphs", "lp_cov-balance-SAVR_TF-TAVR.pdf"),   plot     = cb_final,   height   = 7,   width    = 8 )
