title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: survival_curve() estimates Kaplan-Meier product-limit survival function returns bare ggplot selected plot_type (default "survival"). five companion plots matching SAS %kaplan macro output (PLOTS, PLOTC, PLOTH, PLOTL), plus tidy data frames tables computation, attached attributes (accessible via attr(result, "km_data") etc.). Confidence intervals use logit transform default confidence level 0.95.     Parallel lines across strata indicate proportional hazards.  raw point estimates noisy; add geom_smooth() publication-ready smoothed hazard curve.
code: dta_km <- sample_survival_data(n = 500, seed = 42) head(dta_km) iv_dead  dead iv_opyrs age_at_op 1  3.966736  TRUE 2003.503  58.08251 2 13.217905  TRUE 2008.716  65.37466 3  5.669821  TRUE 1990.072  58.80676 4  0.763838  TRUE 2005.739  66.45205 5  9.463533  TRUE 2006.139  75.84440 6 20.000000 FALSE 1991.461  60.21944 km <- survival_curve(dta_km, alpha = 0.8)  # Bare plot — no scales or labels yet km km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     x     = "Years after Operation",     y     = "Freedom from Death (%)",     title = "Overall Survival"   ) +   annotate("text", x = 1, y = 5,            label = paste0("n = ", nrow(dta_km)),            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") attr(km, "risk_table") strata report_time n.risk 1    All           1    478 2    All           5    412 3    All          10    322 4    All          15    260 5    All          20    207 6    All          25    207 attr(km, "report_table") strata report_time  surv     lower     upper n.risk n.event 1    All           1 0.954 0.9317320 0.9692443    478       1 2    All           5 0.822 0.7859710 0.8530976    412       1 3    All          10 0.642 0.5989814 0.6828473    322       1 4    All          15 0.518 0.4741762 0.5615486    260       1 5    All          20 0.414 0.3715880 0.4577269    207       0 6    All          25 0.414 0.3715880 0.4577269    207       0 km_final <- km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave("../graphs/km_survival.pdf", km_final, width = 8, height = 6) dta_km_s <- sample_survival_data(   n             = 500,   strata_levels = c("Type A", "Type B"),   hazard_ratios = c(1, 1.4),   seed          = 42 )  km_s <- survival_curve(dta_km_s, group_col = "valve_type", alpha = 0.8)  km_s +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)",        title = "Survival by Valve Type") +   theme(legend.position = c(0.15, 0.20)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") survival_curve(dta_km, plot_type = "cumhaz") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Cumulative Hazard H(t)",        title = "Nelson-Aalen Cumulative Hazard") +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") survival_curve(dta_km_s, group_col = "valve_type", plot_type = "loglog") +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   labs(x = "log(Years after Operation)", y = "log(-log S(t))",        title = "Log-Log Survival — Proportional-Hazards Check") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") survival_curve(dta_km, plot_type = "hazard") +   geom_smooth(     aes(x = mid_time, y = hazard, color = strata),     method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = 0.6   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Instantaneous Hazard",        title = "Hazard Rate") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") survival_curve(dta_km, plot_type = "life") +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation",        y = "Restricted Mean Survival (years)",        title = "Integral of Survivorship") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
code: km +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values  = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_y_continuous(     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     x     = "Years after Operation",     y     = "Freedom from Death (%)",     title = "Overall Survival"   ) +   annotate("text", x = 1, y = 5,            label = paste0("n = ", nrow(dta_km)),            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
code: dta_km_s <- sample_survival_data(   n             = 500,   strata_levels = c("Type A", "Type B"),   hazard_ratios = c(1, 1.4),   seed          = 42 )  km_s <- survival_curve(dta_km_s, group_col = "valve_type", alpha = 0.8)  km_s +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Freedom from Death (%)",        title = "Survival by Valve Type") +   theme(legend.position = c(0.15, 0.20)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: raw point estimates noisy; add geom_smooth() publication-ready smoothed hazard curve.
code: survival_curve(dta_km, plot_type = "hazard") +   geom_smooth(     aes(x = mid_time, y = hazard, color = strata),     method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = 0.6   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(All = "steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Instantaneous Hazard",        title = "Hazard Rate") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: hvtiPlotR package provides eda_plot() exploratory data analysis variables dataset reference time axis. replicates Function_DataPlotting() workflow tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots.R tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R, replacing base-R graphics composable ggplot2 objects. Three helper functions support workflow: eda_classify_var() — detects whether column continuous ("Cont"), numeric-categorical ("Cat_Num"), character-categorical ("Cat_Char"), matching UniqueLimit logic template. eda_select_vars() — subsets reorders columns name space-separated string, replacing Order_Variables() / Mod_Data <- dta[, Order_Var] pattern varnames template. sample_eda_data() — generates reproducible mixed-type dataset demonstration. eda_plot() always returns bare ggplot object. Colour scales, axis labels, annotations, hvti_theme() added caller. Numeric 0/1 columns classified "Cat_Num". NA values appear explicit "(Missing)" fill level can coloured scale_fill_manual(). y_label argument sets plot title fill-legend name place raw column name.  Setting show_percent = TRUE switches geom_bar() position = "fill".    Continuous columns produce scatter plot LOESS smoother overlay. y_col NA, rug mark drawn x-axis.  eda_select_vars() subsets columns name, matching Var_CatList / Var_ContList + Order_Variables() workflow. named vector whose names column names whose values human-readable labels drives single lapply() loop.        gridExtra::marrangeGrob() arranges multiple plots grid ggsave() writes page separate PDF file.
code: dta_eda <- sample_eda_data(n = 300, seed = 42) head(dta_eda) year op_years male cabg nyha valve_morph   ef lv_mass peak_grad 1 2005     0.48    1    0    2   Tricuspid 66.3    97.6      31.0 2 2009     4.44    1    0    2   Unicuspid 52.0   121.1      38.0 3 2005     0.06    0    0    2   Tricuspid   NA   129.7      25.2 4 2013     8.32    1    0    3    Bicuspid 49.8   131.4      52.5 5 2014     9.87    1    0    4   Tricuspid   NA   146.3        NA 6 2008     3.92    1    1    1    Bicuspid 20.0   148.0      45.1 # Inspect auto-detected types for each column sapply(dta_eda, eda_classify_var) year    op_years        male        cabg        nyha valve_morph      "Cont"      "Cont"   "Cat_Num"   "Cat_Num"   "Cat_Num"  "Cat_Char"          ef     lv_mass   peak_grad      "Cont"      "Cont"      "Cont" eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "year", y_col = "male",          y_label = "Sex") +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("0" = "steelblue", "1" = "firebrick", "(Missing)" = "grey80"),     labels = c("0" = "Female", "1" = "Male", "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "year", y_col = "cabg",          y_label = "Concomitant CABG", show_percent = TRUE) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("0" = "grey70", "1" = "steelblue", "(Missing)" = "grey90"),     labels = c("0" = "No CABG", "1" = "CABG", "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "year", y_col = "nyha",          y_label = "Preoperative NYHA Class") +   scale_fill_brewer(     palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,     labels  = c("1" = "I", "2" = "II", "3" = "III", "4" = "IV",                 "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name    = "NYHA"   ) +   scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "year", y_col = "valve_morph",          y_label = "Valve Morphology") +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(Bicuspid   = "steelblue",                Tricuspid  = "firebrick",                Unicuspid  = "goldenrod3",                "(Missing)" = "grey80"),     name = "Morphology"   ) +   scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") eda_plot(dta_eda, x_col = "op_years", y_col = "ef",          y_label = "Ejection Fraction (%)") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(20, 80), breaks = seq(20, 80, 20)) +   labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year",        caption = "Tick marks on x-axis: observations with missing EF") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") bin_vars <- c(male = "Sex (Male)", cabg = "Concomitant CABG") sub_bin  <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("year", names(bin_vars)))  p_bin <- lapply(names(bin_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_bin, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = bin_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", direction = -1, name = NULL) +     scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_bin[[1]] p_bin[[2]] cat_vars <- c(nyha        = "NYHA Class",               valve_morph = "Valve Morphology") sub_cat <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("year", names(cat_vars)))  p_cat <- lapply(names(cat_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cat, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = cat_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = NULL) +     scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cat[[1]] p_cat[[2]] cont_vars <- c(ef        = "Ejection Fraction (%)",                lv_mass   = "LV Mass Index (g/m\u00b2)",                peak_grad = "Peak Gradient (mmHg)") sub_cont <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("op_years", names(cont_vars)))  p_cont <- lapply(names(cont_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cont, x_col = "op_years", y_col = cn,            y_label = cont_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +     scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +     labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cont[[1]] p_cont[[2]] p_cont[[3]] all_plots <- c(p_bin, p_cat, p_cont) per_page  <- 9L  # 3 x 3 grid  for (pg in seq(1, length(all_plots), by = per_page)) {   idx  <- seq(pg, min(pg + per_page - 1L, length(all_plots)))   grob <- gridExtra::marrangeGrob(all_plots[idx], nrow = 3, ncol = 3)   ggsave(     filename = sprintf("../graphs/eda_page%02d.pdf", ceiling(pg / per_page)),     plot     = grob,     width    = 14,     height   = 14   ) }

what: Variable selection and labels (varnames template pattern)
title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: eda_select_vars() subsets columns name, matching Var_CatList / Var_ContList + Order_Variables() workflow. named vector whose names column names whose values human-readable labels drives single lapply() loop.
code: bin_vars <- c(male = "Sex (Male)", cabg = "Concomitant CABG") sub_bin  <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("year", names(bin_vars)))  p_bin <- lapply(names(bin_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_bin, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = bin_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", direction = -1, name = NULL) +     scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_bin[[1]] p_bin[[2]] cat_vars <- c(nyha        = "NYHA Class",               valve_morph = "Valve Morphology") sub_cat <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("year", names(cat_vars)))  p_cat <- lapply(names(cat_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cat, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = cat_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = NULL) +     scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cat[[1]] p_cat[[2]] cont_vars <- c(ef        = "Ejection Fraction (%)",                lv_mass   = "LV Mass Index (g/m\u00b2)",                peak_grad = "Peak Gradient (mmHg)") sub_cont <- eda_select_vars(dta_eda, c("op_years", names(cont_vars)))  p_cont <- lapply(names(cont_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cont, x_col = "op_years", y_col = cn,            y_label = cont_vars[[cn]]) +     scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +     scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +     labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cont[[1]] p_cont[[2]] p_cont[[3]]

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: alluvial_plot() produces alluvial (Sankey-style) diagram using ggalluvial. row input unique combination axis values associated patient count; flows drawn proportional count. Ports tp.dp.female_bicus_preAR_sankey.R.
code: dta_al  <- sample_alluvial_data(n = 300, seed = 42) axes    <- c("pre_ar", "procedure", "post_ar") head(dta_al) pre_ar   procedure post_ar freq 1     Mild      Repair    Mild    3 2 Moderate      Repair    Mild    7 4   Severe      Repair    Mild    5 5     Mild Replacement    Mild    3 6 Moderate Replacement    Mild   25 8   Severe Replacement    Mild   17 alluvial_plot(dta_al, axes = axes, y_col = "freq") alluvial_plot(dta_al, axes = axes, y_col = "freq",               fill_col = "pre_ar") +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     name = "Pre-op AR"   ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     guide = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = 1:3,     labels = c("Pre-op AR", "Procedure", "Post-op AR"),     expand = c(0.05, 0.05)   ) +   labs(y = "Patients (n)",        title = "AV Regurgitation: Pre- to Post-operative") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") alluvial_plot(   dta_al,   axes        = c("pre_ar", "post_ar"),   y_col       = "freq",   fill_col    = "pre_ar",   axis_labels = c("Pre-operative", "Post-operative") ) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,                     name = "AR Grade") +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,                       guide = "none") +   annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = 250,            label = "Improvement after surgery",            size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +   labs(y = "Patients (n)",        title = "AV Regurgitation Before and After Surgery") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_al <- alluvial_plot(dta_al, axes = axes, y_col = "freq",                       fill_col = "pre_ar") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1, guide = "none") +   labs(y = "Patients (n)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave("../graphs/alluvial.pdf", p_al, width = 8, height = 6)

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: cluster_sankey_plot() draws Sankey diagram showing patients flow labelled clusters number clusters K increases. ports PAM cluster stability figure HVTI clustering analysis pipeline. column represents one value K (default K = 2 9); band shows fraction patients whose assignment changes consecutive K values. Node labels show cluster letter count. Requires ggsankey (CRAN):  Replace default Set1 colours fully custom named vector. Names must match node labels data.  Pass shorter cluster_cols vector show range K.
code: remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey") dta_san <- sample_cluster_sankey_data(n = 300, seed = 42) head(dta_san) C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9 1  B  B  B  B  F  F  H  H 2  B  B  B  B  F  F  H  H 3  A  A  A  A  A  A  A  A 4  A  A  A  A  A  G  G  G 5  B  B  D  D  D  D  D  D 6  B  B  B  B  F  F  F  F table(dta_san$C9) B  F  H  D  I  C  E  G  A 59 39 22 26 11 42 38 22 41 cluster_sankey_plot(dta_san) +   labs(title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 9") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") my_cols <- c(   A = "#1f77b4", B = "#ff7f0e", C = "#2ca02c", D = "#d62728",   E = "#9467bd", F = "#8c564b", G = "#e377c2", H = "#7f7f7f",   I = "#bcbd22" ) cluster_sankey_plot(dta_san, node_colours = my_cols) +   labs(title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 9") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") cluster_sankey_plot(dta_san, cluster_cols = paste0("C", 2:6)) +   labs(title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 6") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_san <- cluster_sankey_plot(dta_san) +   labs(title = "PAM Cluster Stability") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave("../graphs/sankey_clusters.pdf", p_san, width = 8, height = 5)

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: hazard_plot() plots pre-computed parametric survival, hazard, cumulative-hazard curves fitted Weibull (parametric) model, optional Kaplan-Meier empirical overlay population life-table reference. ports entire tp.hp.dead.* SAS template family. input data comes two sources map directly SAS output: common tp.hp.dead.sas output: smooth parametric survival confidence band plus discrete KM empirical circles error bars.  Switching estimate_col "hazard" (matching CI columns) shows instantaneous hazard rate (%/year) instead survival.  Used readmission repeated-event analyses (tp.hp.event.weighted.sas, tp.hp.repeated_events.sas). "cumhaz" column equals -log(S) * 100.  Pass group_col compare two groups (tp.hp.dead.tkdn.stratified.sas).  tp.hp.dead.age_with_population_life_table.sas tp.hp.dead.uslife.stratifed.sas overlay US population life-table survival (dashed lines) age-stratified study curves. Pass life-table data frame reference set ref_group_col vary linetype age group.
code: dat_hp  <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_hp  <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6) head(dat_hp) time survival surv_lower surv_upper    hazard haz_lower haz_upper 1 0.01000000 99.99558   99.93731        100 0.6629126 0.3996474  1.099602 2 0.03002004 99.97702   99.84413        100 1.1485818 0.6924408  1.905203 3 0.05004008 99.95054   99.75561        100 1.4829116 0.8939968  2.459770 4 0.07006012 99.91808   99.66720        100 1.7546549 1.0578216  2.910523 5 0.09008016 99.88059   99.57770        100 1.9896233 1.1994760  3.300275 6 0.11010020 99.83868   99.48662        100 2.1996335 1.3260840  3.648628        cumhaz cumhaz_lower cumhaz_upper 1 0.004419417            0    0.5871202 2 0.022986980            0    1.3519229 3 0.049470012            0    1.9990187 4 0.081954223            0    2.5912321 5 0.119483723            0    3.1493081 6 0.161453396            0    3.6834317 hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_hp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col = "hazard",   lower_col    = "haz_lower",   upper_col    = "haz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%/yr")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Hazard (%/year)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col  = "cumhaz",   lower_col     = "cumhaz_lower",   upper_col     = "cumhaz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Cumulative Hazard (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_strat <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 400, time_max = 10,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) emp_strat <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 400, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) )  hazard_plot(   dat_strat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_strat,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Esophagostomy", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_age <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 12,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65\u201380" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) emp_age <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 12, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65\u201380" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) lt <- sample_life_table(   age_groups = c("<65", "65\u201380", "\u226580"),   age_mids   = c(55, 72, 85),   time_max   = 12 )  hazard_plot(   dat_age,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp_age,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65\u201380" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Age group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65\u201380" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: survival_difference_plot() plots difference S_2(t) - S_1(t) two groups time, optional confidence band. ports tp.hp.dead.life-gained.sas (HAZDIFL macro output).  Combine several two-group differences one long data frame use group_col overlay .
code: diff_dat <- sample_survival_difference_data(   n      = 500,   groups = c("Control" = 1.0, "Treatment" = 0.70) ) head(diff_dat) time  difference  diff_lower diff_upper group1_surv group2_surv 1 0.01000000 0.001831068 -0.03739885 0.04106099    99.99558    99.99741 2 0.03002004 0.009522643 -0.08737593 0.10642122    99.97702    99.98654 3 0.05004008 0.020489267 -0.13072627 0.17170481    99.95054    99.97103 4 0.07006012 0.033934691 -0.17121839 0.23908777    99.91808    99.95201 5 0.09008016 0.049459769 -0.21006489 0.30898443    99.88059    99.93005 6 0.11010020 0.066810699 -0.24784775 0.38146915    99.83868    99.90549 survival_difference_plot(   diff_dat,   lower_col = "diff_lower",   upper_col = "diff_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",                       colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-5, 40),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") d1 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("Medical Mgmt" = 1.0, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 1L ) d1$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs Medical Mgmt"  d2 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("TA-TAVR" = 0.90, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 2L ) d2$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs TA-TAVR"  d3 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("AVR" = 0.80, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 3L ) d3$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs AVR"  survival_difference_plot(rbind(d1, d2, d3),                          group_col = "comparison") +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", guide = "none") +   ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",                       colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: trends_plot() ports pattern five SAS/R templates: function accepts patient-level data computes annual summaries (mean median) internally. returns bare ggplot object. Compose scale_colour_*, scale_shape_*, scale_x_continuous(), scale_y_continuous(), coord_cartesian(), labs(), annotate(), hvti_theme(). SAS template plots annual case volume operation year axisx order=(1968 2000 4) axisy order=(0 10 2).  age figure uses x-axis axisy order=(30 70 10).     VSD template plots multiple binary outcomes (cardiogenic shock %, pre-op IABP %, inotropes %) axes: axisx order=(1970 2000 10), axisy order=(0 100 10). outcome becomes group.  mitral valve template uses patient age (year) x-axis: axisx order=(25 85 10), axisy order=(0 100 20). Pass real data age column set x_col = "age". example uses sample data’s year column relabelled illustration.  Four repair categories short study period (1990–1999): SAS template uses axisx order=(1990 1999 1) fine year breaks.  Continuous outcome larger y range: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)), coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1995, 2016), ylim = c(0, 200)).   Template: coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 20)), annotate("text", 1995, 18, label="Trend: Hospital Length Stay", size=4.5).

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: spaghetti_plot() ports pattern tp.dp.spaghetti.echo.R: one trajectory line per subject time, optional stratification grouping variable optional LOESS smooth overlay. original template covers nine figures — unstratified sex-stratified variants three echo outcomes (AV mean gradient, AV area, DVI) plus ordinal MV regurgitation grade plot. returns bare ggplot object. Compose scale_colour_manual(), scale_x_continuous(), scale_y_continuous(), coord_cartesian(), labs(), hvti_theme(). Template: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 80, 20)), coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)).  Template: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 30, 10)), coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 30)).  Template: scale_color_manual(breaks = c("0", "1"), values=c("red", "blue")). quick-start template header uses modernised "firebrick" / "steelblue" equivalents.  Template: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 5, 1)), coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 5)), ylab('AV Area (EOA) (cm^2)').  Template: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 1.25, 0.25)), coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1.25)), ylab('DVI').  Template: scale_y_continuous(labels=c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")), coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6), ylim = c(0, 3)), two colour groups early (blue) late (red2) cohorts.   Template uses wid = 11, hei = 8.5 pdf().
code: # groups mirrors the Female/Male sex stratification in the template (MALE column) dta_sp <- sample_spaghetti_data(   n_patients = 150,   max_obs    = 6,   groups     = c(Female = 0.45, Male = 0.55),   seed       = 42L ) head(dta_sp) id time value  group 1  1 0.44 22.12 Female 2  1 0.67 27.20 Female 3  1 0.91 29.24 Female 4  1 1.29 18.95 Female 5  1 1.96 24.00 Female 6  2 2.04 25.16 Female spaghetti_plot(dta_sp) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta_sp) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 30, 10)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 30)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Area (EOA) (cm\u00b2)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1.25, 0.25)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 1.25)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "DVI") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_ord        <- dta_sp dta_ord$value  <- round(pmin(3, pmax(0, dta_sp$value / 12))) levels(dta_ord$group) <- c("Early", "Late")  spaghetti_plot(   dta_ord,   colour_col = "group",   y_labels   = c(None = 0, Mild = 1, Moderate = 2, Severe = 3) ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Early = "steelblue", Late = "red2"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 6, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6), ylim = c(0, 3)) +   labs(x = "Years after Procedure", y = "MV Regurgitation Grade") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group", add_smooth = TRUE) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_sp <- spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(here::here("graphs", "mp.amngrd_profile.pdf"),        p_sp, width = 11, height = 8.5)

code: spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Area (EOA) (cm\u00b2)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: Template: scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(0, 1.25, 0.25)), coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1.25)), ylab('DVI').

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: Template: scale_y_continuous(labels=c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")), coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6), ylim = c(0, 3)), two colour groups early (blue) late (red2) cohorts.
code: dta_ord        <- dta_sp dta_ord$value  <- round(pmin(3, pmax(0, dta_sp$value / 12))) levels(dta_ord$group) <- c("Early", "Late")  spaghetti_plot(   dta_ord,   colour_col = "group",   y_labels   = c(None = 0, Mild = 1, Moderate = 2, Severe = 3) ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Early = "steelblue", Late = "red2"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 6, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6), ylim = c(0, 3)) +   labs(x = "Years after Procedure", y = "MV Regurgitation Grade") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
code: spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group", add_smooth = TRUE) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: Template uses wid = 11, hei = 8.5 pdf().
code: p_sp <- spaghetti_plot(dta_sp, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(here::here("graphs", "mp.amngrd_profile.pdf"),        p_sp, width = 11, height = 8.5)

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: nonparametric_curve_plot() visualises pre-computed average curves two-phase nonparametric temporal trend model — R equivalent SAS tp.np.*.avrg_curv.* tp.np.*.u.trend.* template family. function accepts SAS mean_curv / boots_ci datasets exported CSV read read.csv(). returns bare ggplot object composition scale_colour_*, labs(), hvti_theme().  shaded ribbon shows 68 % bootstrap CI (one standard error), matching cll_p68/clu_p68 SAS template. Switch ci_level = 0.95 sample_nonparametric_curve_data() 95 % CI bands.
code: curve_dat <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n            = 500,   time_max     = 12,   outcome_type = "probability",   ci_level     = 0.68       # 68 % CI — one SE, matching SAS cll_p68/clu_p68 ) pts_dat <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(n = 500, time_max = 12) head(curve_dat) time  estimate     lower     upper 1 0.05000000 0.2395042 0.2148392 0.2660414 2 0.05055219 0.2395916 0.2149201 0.2661351 3 0.05111048 0.2396798 0.2150019 0.2662297 4 0.05167493 0.2397690 0.2150845 0.2663253 5 0.05224562 0.2398592 0.2151680 0.2664220 6 0.05282261 0.2399503 0.2152524 0.2665196 nonparametric_curve_plot(   curve_data  = curve_dat,   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = pts_dat ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 12),     breaks = seq(0, 12, 2),     labels = function(x) paste(x, "yr")   ) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Follow-up (years)", y = "Prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") curve_grp <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n      = 400,   time_max = 7,   groups   = c("Ozaki" = 0.7, "CE-Pericardial" = 1.3),   outcome_type = "continuous",   ci_level = 0.68 ) pts_grp <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(   n = 400, time_max = 7,   groups = c("Ozaki" = 0.7, "CE-Pericardial" = 1.3),   outcome_type = "continuous" )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   curve_data  = curve_grp,   group_col   = "group",   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = pts_grp ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "steelblue", "CE-Pericardial" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "steelblue", "CE-Pericardial" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = 0:7) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Follow-up (years)",     y = "AV Peak Gradient (mmHg)"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   ggplot2::theme(legend.position = c(0.15, 0.85)) p_np <- nonparametric_curve_plot(curve_dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Follow-up (years)", y = "Prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Manuscript PDF ggsave(here::here("graphs", "np_afib_prevalence.pdf"),        p_np, width = 11, height = 8.5)  # Editable PowerPoint save_ppt(p_np,          template   = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),          powerpoint = here::here("graphs", "np_afib_prevalence.pptx"))

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: nonparametric_ordinal_plot() plots pre-computed grade-specific probability curves cumulative proportional-odds model — R equivalent tp.np.*.ordinal.* SAS templates (e.g. TR grade prevalence, AR severity). SAS predict dataset stores one column per grade (p0, p1, p2, p3). calling function, reshape wide long format:  line one grade level. Points show binned data summary values (analogous SAS means dataset smntr0–smntr3 columns). analyses collapse higher grades (e.g. Moderate + Severe combined), subset re-label plotting:
code: library(tidyr) long <- pivot_longer(   predict_wide,   cols      = c(p0, p1, p2, p3),   names_to  = "grade",   values_to = "estimate" ) ord_dat <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(   n            = 800,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe") ) ord_pts <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(   n            = 800,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe") ) head(ord_dat) time  estimate grade 1 0.01000000 0.6276258  None 2 0.01012532 0.6276898  None 3 0.01025221 0.6277545  None 4 0.01038069 0.6278200  None 5 0.01051078 0.6278864  None 6 0.01064250 0.6279535  None nonparametric_ordinal_plot(   curve_data  = ord_dat,   data_points = ord_pts ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(       "None"     = "grey40",       "Mild"     = "steelblue",       "Moderate" = "darkorange",       "Severe"   = "firebrick"     ),     name = "AR Grade"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5), breaks = 0:5) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Follow-up (years)",     y = "Grade prevalence (%)"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   ggplot2::theme(legend.position = c(0.75, 0.6)) ord_collapsed <- ord_dat # Merge Moderate and Severe into one level ord_collapsed <- subset(ord_collapsed, grade %in% c("None", "Mild", "Moderate/Severe"))  # If your data has Moderate and Severe as separate rows, combine with: # library(dplyr) # ord_collapsed <- ord_dat |> #   dplyr::filter(grade %in% c("Moderate", "Severe")) |> #   dplyr::summarise(estimate = sum(estimate), .by = time) |> #   dplyr::mutate(grade = "Moderate/Severe") |> #   dplyr::bind_rows(dplyr::filter(ord_dat, grade %in% c("None", "Mild")))  # Show three-level version using only two grades from sample data ord_two <- subset(ord_dat, grade %in% c("None", "Severe"))  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(curve_data = ord_two) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c("None" = "steelblue", "Severe" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5), breaks = 0:5) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),                                labels = scales::percent) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Follow-up (years)", y = "Grade prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_ord <- nonparametric_ordinal_plot(ord_dat, data_points = ord_pts) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "TR Grade") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Follow-up (years)", y = "Grade prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(here::here("graphs", "np_tr_ordinal.pdf"),        p_ord, width = 11, height = 8.5)

code: ord_collapsed <- ord_dat # Merge Moderate and Severe into one level ord_collapsed <- subset(ord_collapsed, grade %in% c("None", "Mild", "Moderate/Severe"))  # If your data has Moderate and Severe as separate rows, combine with: # library(dplyr) # ord_collapsed <- ord_dat |> #   dplyr::filter(grade %in% c("Moderate", "Severe")) |> #   dplyr::summarise(estimate = sum(estimate), .by = time) |> #   dplyr::mutate(grade = "Moderate/Severe") |> #   dplyr::bind_rows(dplyr::filter(ord_dat, grade %in% c("None", "Mild")))  # Show three-level version using only two grades from sample data ord_two <- subset(ord_dat, grade %in% c("None", "Severe"))  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(curve_data = ord_two) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c("None" = "steelblue", "Severe" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5), breaks = 0:5) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),                                labels = scales::percent) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Follow-up (years)", y = "Grade prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: longitudinal_counts_plot() longitudinal_counts_table() reproduce two-panel layout tp.dp.longitudinal_patients_measures.*: grouped bar chart patients measurements follow-window, paired numeric summary table . Compose two panels patchwork. Input pre-aggregated long-format data — one row per (time window, series) combination. Use sample_longitudinal_counts_data() derive patient-level data, build registry data.
code: lc_dat <- sample_longitudinal_counts_data(n_patients = 300, seed = 42L) lc_dat time_label       series count 1     ≥0 Days     Patients    19 2    ≥1 Month     Patients    47 3   ≥3 Months     Patients    49 4   ≥6 Months     Patients   100 5     ≥1 Year     Patients   159 6    ≥2 Years     Patients   101 7  ≥2.5 Years     Patients   276 8     ≥0 Days Measurements    19 9    ≥1 Month Measurements    50 10  ≥3 Months Measurements    56 11  ≥6 Months Measurements   118 12    ≥1 Year Measurements   217 13   ≥2 Years Measurements   113 14 ≥2.5 Years Measurements   620 p_lc_bar <- longitudinal_counts_plot(lc_dat) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(Patients = "steelblue", Measurements = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(     breaks = seq(0, 2000, 500),     expand = c(0, 0)   ) +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 2200)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = "Count (n)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") +   ggplot2::theme(legend.position = c(0.85, 0.85))  p_lc_bar p_lc_tbl <- longitudinal_counts_table(lc_dat) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Patients = "steelblue", Measurements = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  p_lc_tbl library(patchwork)  p_lc_bar / p_lc_tbl +   patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1)) combined <- p_lc_bar / p_lc_tbl +   patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1))  ggsave(here::here("graphs", "longitudinal_participation.pdf"),        combined, width = 11, height = 6)  # PowerPoint (bar chart only — patchwork composites may need ggsave first) save_ppt(p_lc_bar,          template   = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),          powerpoint = here::here("graphs", "longitudinal_participation.pptx"))

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: upset_plot() wraps ComplexUpset::upset() visualise surgical procedure co-occurrences set membership data. ports pattern tp.complexUpset.R, replacing hard-coded colours scale_fill_* composition hard-coded themes hvti_theme(). Apply theme panels using patchwork & operator (+):
code: upset_plot(dta, intersect = sets) & hvti_theme("manuscript") sets <- c("AV_Replacement", "AV_Repair", "MV_Replacement", "MV_Repair",           "TV_Repair", "Aorta", "CABG")  upset_dta <- sample_upset_data(n = 400, seed = 42) head(upset_dta) AV_Replacement AV_Repair MV_Replacement MV_Repair TV_Repair Aorta  CABG 1          FALSE     FALSE          FALSE      TRUE     FALSE FALSE FALSE 2          FALSE     FALSE          FALSE      TRUE     FALSE FALSE FALSE 3          FALSE     FALSE          FALSE     FALSE     FALSE FALSE  TRUE 4          FALSE      TRUE          FALSE     FALSE     FALSE FALSE FALSE 5          FALSE     FALSE           TRUE     FALSE      TRUE FALSE FALSE 6           TRUE     FALSE          FALSE     FALSE     FALSE FALSE  TRUE colSums(upset_dta) AV_Replacement      AV_Repair MV_Replacement      MV_Repair      TV_Repair            127             50             56             49             42          Aorta           CABG             56            147 upset_plot(upset_dta, intersect = sets) &   hvti_theme("manuscript") upset_plot(   upset_dta,   intersect = sets,   base_annotations = list(     "Intersection size" = ComplexUpset::intersection_size(       mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill = "n")     ) +       ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(         values = c("n" = "steelblue"),         guide  = "none"       ) +       ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)")   ) ) &   hvti_theme("manuscript") upset_dta$era <- ifelse(seq_len(nrow(upset_dta)) <= 200, "Early", "Recent")  upset_plot(   upset_dta,   intersect = sets,   base_annotations = list(     "Intersection size" = ComplexUpset::intersection_size(       counts  = FALSE,       mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill = era)     ) +       ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(         values = c("Early" = "grey60", "Recent" = "steelblue"),         name   = "Era"       ) +       ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)")   ) ) &   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_upset <- upset_plot(upset_dta, intersect = sets) & hvti_theme("manuscript")  # UpSet plots are patchwork composites — use ggsave() via ggplot2 ggplot2::ggsave(here::here("graphs", "procedure_cooccurrence.pdf"),                 p_upset, width = 11, height = 6)

title: hvtiPlotR Plot Functions
text: make_footnote() writes small annotation bottom-right corner current graphics device using grid. Call printing plot mark figure work--progress analysis. publication-ready output, simply omit call — plot unchanged.  Pass string text set timestamp = FALSE stable label.  ggsave() call writes ggplot object directly — make_footnote() never called, saved file clean.
code: # During analysis                   # For publication print(p)                             ggsave("fig1.pdf", p, ...) make_footnote("R/analysis.R")        # <- no footnote call dta_fn <- sample_hazard_data(n = 300, time_max = 10) emp_fn <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 300, time_max = 10, n_bins = 5)  p_draft <- hazard_plot(   dta_fn,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_fn,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  print(p_draft) make_footnote("vignettes/plot-functions.qmd") print(p_draft) make_footnote(   text      = paste("J. Ehrlinger |", Sys.Date()),   timestamp = FALSE,   prefix    = "" ) ggsave("../graphs/fig1_survival.pdf", p_draft, width = 11, height = 8.5)

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: guide maps every SAS template HVTI statistics group template library R equivalent hvtiPlotR package. know SAS template name (e.g., tp.np.afib.ivwristm.avrg_curv.binary.sas), look table jump corresponding section working R example. guide organized template family (two-letter prefix tp.). New ports added document become available. ggplot2 builds plots layers. Instead one macro call many color=, xaxis=, footnote= options, chain + operations: hvti_theme() replaces SAS device= / style options. Use "dark_ppt" / "ppt" PowerPoint slides, "light_ppt" light-background slides, "manuscript" journal figures, "poster" conference posters. Functions return ggplot object; display . Call plot object top level (use print()) render , save ggsave() / save_ppt(). Pre-fitted models, raw data. functions accept output statistical model (curve datasets, probability estimates) rather individual patient records — mirroring SAS workflow %decompos() %kaplan computes estimates separate template step produces figure.
code: nonparametric_curve_plot(dat, ...) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "gray40")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:12) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: tp.np.* templates share common SAS workflow: Fit patient-specific temporal profiles %decompos(). Average profiles across patients PROC SUMMARY → mean_curv dataset. Optionally compute bootstrap confidence intervals → boots_ci dataset. Compute binned patient-level summaries (deciles/quintiles) → means dataset. Call plotting template. R port replaces step 5. Steps 1–4 still run SAS; export resulting datasets CSV read R: Ports: tp.np.afib.ivwristm.avrg_curv.binary.sas, tp.np.afib.ivwristm.pt_specific.binary.sas, tp.np.tr.icdpr.avg_curv.sas  Add 68% CI bands (one standard error; matches SAS boots_ci cll_p68 / clu_p68):  Add binned data summary points (matches SAS means dataset):  Ports: tp.np.fev.double.univariate.continuous.sas, tp.np.fev.u.trend.continuous.sas continuous outcomes (FEV1, AV peak gradient) set outcome_type = "continuous". y-axis label scale change accordingly; everything else identical.  Ports: tp.np.afib.mult.avrg_curv.binary.sas, tp.np.afib.mult.pt_spec.binary.sas, tp.np.avpkgrad_ozak_ind_mtwt.sas, tp.np.fev.multivariate.continuous.sas Provide named vector groups generating sample data, set group_col supplying curve data.  Ports: tp.np.afib.ivwristm.pt_spec_phases.binary.sas, tp.np.tr.ivecho.u.phases.sas Phase plots separate early (bell-shaped, incomplete hazard) late (Weibull CDF, complete hazard) components. Supply group_col whose levels label phases.  Port: tp.np.z0axdpo.continuous.bmi_xaxis.sas BMI another continuous covariate (rather time) x-axis, function signature identical — simply pass covariate column name x_col.  Port: tp.np.tr.ivecho.average_curv.ordinal.sas Ordinal templates (TR grade, AR grade) use nonparametric_ordinal_plot(). critical migration step reshaping SAS wide-format predict dataset (one column per grade) long format. SAS reshape (reading R): R reshape:  Port: tp.np.po_ar.u_multi.ordinal.sas  Port: tp.np.tr.ivecho.independence.sas examine single grade isolation, filter long-format curve passing function:  Port: tp.np.tr.ivecho.u.phases.sas Phase-decomposed ordinal plots combine phase labels grade colours. Create figure plotting grade-specific curves annotating early vs. late phase regions:
code: curve_data <- read.csv("mean_curv.csv")   # or boots_ci if CI bands are needed data_pts   <- read.csv("means.csv")       # optional binned data points dat     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n            = 500,   time_max     = 12,   outcome_type = "probability" ) dat_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(n = 500, time_max = 12)  # Minimal plot nonparametric_curve_plot(dat) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation Prevalence"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat,   lower_col = "lower",   upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation — 68% CI"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat,   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_pts ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation with Binned Observations"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_cont     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n            = 400,   time_max     = 10,   outcome_type = "continuous" ) dat_cont_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(   n            = 400,   time_max     = 10,   outcome_type = "continuous" )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_cont,   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_cont_pts ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 4)) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = expression(FEV[1] ~ (L)),     title = "FEV\u2081 Temporal Trend"   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 9, y = 3.5,     label = "68% CI band",     hjust = 1, size = 3   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") grp_def <- c("Ozaki" = 0.7, "CE-Pericardial" = 1.1, "Homograft" = 1.4) dat_grp     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n        = 600,   time_max = 12,   groups   = grp_def ) dat_grp_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(   n        = 600,   time_max = 12,   groups   = grp_def )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_grp,   group_col   = "group",   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_grp_pts ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "#003087", "CE-Pericardial" = "#CC0000", "Homograft" = "#666666")   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "#003087", "CE-Pericardial" = "#CC0000", "Homograft" = "#666666")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Prevalence (%)",     colour = "Valve type",     fill   = "Valve type",     title  = "Atrial Fibrillation by Valve Type"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_phase <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n      = 500,   groups = c("Early phase" = 1.5, "Late phase" = 0.6, "Overall" = 1.0) )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_phase,   group_col = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Early phase" = "#CC0000",                "Late phase"  = "#003087",                "Overall"     = "black")   ) +   scale_linetype_manual(     values = c("Early phase" = "dashed",                "Late phase"  = "dashed",                "Overall"     = "solid")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Prevalence (%)",     colour = NULL,     title  = "AF — Early and Late Phase Decomposition"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") # Simulate covariate (BMI) x-axis data set.seed(42) n_pts  <- 300 bmi    <- seq(18, 45, length.out = n_pts) est    <- plogis(-3 + 0.08 * bmi) se     <- sqrt(est * (1 - est) / 50) bmi_curve <- data.frame(   bmi   = bmi,   est   = est,   lower = pmax(0, est - qnorm(0.84) * se),   upper = pmin(1, est + qnorm(0.84) * se) )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   bmi_curve,   x_col        = "bmi",   estimate_col = "est",   lower_col    = "lower",   upper_col    = "upper" ) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = seq(18, 45, 3),     limits = c(18, 45)   ) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = expression(BMI ~ (kg/m^2)),     y     = "Estimated Probability",     title = "Outcome Probability vs. BMI at Operation"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") /* SAS wide format: p0, p1, p2, p3 */ data predict_wide;   set predict; run; library(tidyr) long <- pivot_longer(   predict_wide,   cols      = c(p0, p1, p2, p3),   names_to  = "grade",   values_to = "estimate" ) ord_labels <- c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe") dat_ord     <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(   n            = 1000,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = ord_labels ) dat_ord_pts <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(   n            = 1000,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = ord_labels )  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(   dat_ord,   grade_col   = "grade",   data_points = dat_ord_pts ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(       "None"     = "#003087",       "Mild"     = "#55A51C",       "Moderate" = "#FFA500",       "Severe"   = "#CC0000"     )   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Grade probability",     colour = "TR Grade",     title  = "Tricuspid Regurgitation Grade"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ar1 <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(seed = 1) dat_ar2 <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(seed = 99)  dat_ar1$scenario <- "Before repair" dat_ar2$scenario <- "After repair" combined <- rbind(dat_ar1, dat_ar2)  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(combined, grade_col = "grade") +   facet_wrap(~scenario) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years",     y      = "Grade probability",     colour = "AR Grade",     title  = "AR Grade — Before vs. After Repair"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ind    <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(n = 800) pts_ind    <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(n = 800) grade_2    <- dat_ind[dat_ind$grade == "Grade 2", ] dp_grade_2 <- pts_ind[pts_ind$grade == "Grade 2", ]  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(   grade_2,   grade_col   = "grade",   data_points = dp_grade_2 ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Grade 2" = "#CC0000")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 0.5),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Probability",     title = "Probability of TR Grade 2"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ph <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(n = 800, seed = 7)  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(dat_ph, grade_col = "grade") +   annotate("rect",     xmin = 0, xmax = 2,  ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,     fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.07   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 1, y = 0.95, label = "Early\nphase",     size = 3, colour = "steelblue", fontface = "italic"   ) +   annotate("rect",     xmin = 2, xmax = 5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,     fill = "tomato", alpha = 0.07   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 3.5, y = 0.95, label = "Late\nphase",     size = 3, colour = "tomato", fontface = "italic"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Grade probability",     colour = "TR Grade",     title  = "TR Grade — Early and Late Phase"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.np.afib.ivwristm.avrg_curv.binary.sas, tp.np.afib.ivwristm.pt_specific.binary.sas, tp.np.tr.icdpr.avg_curv.sas  Add 68% CI bands (one standard error; matches SAS boots_ci cll_p68 / clu_p68):  Add binned data summary points (matches SAS means dataset):
code: dat     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n            = 500,   time_max     = 12,   outcome_type = "probability" ) dat_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(n = 500, time_max = 12)  # Minimal plot nonparametric_curve_plot(dat) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation Prevalence"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat,   lower_col = "lower",   upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation — 68% CI"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat,   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_pts ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Prevalence (%)",     title = "Atrial Fibrillation with Binned Observations"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.np.fev.double.univariate.continuous.sas, tp.np.fev.u.trend.continuous.sas continuous outcomes (FEV1, AV peak gradient) set outcome_type = "continuous". y-axis label scale change accordingly; everything else identical.
code: dat_cont     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n            = 400,   time_max     = 10,   outcome_type = "continuous" ) dat_cont_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(   n            = 400,   time_max     = 10,   outcome_type = "continuous" )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_cont,   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_cont_pts ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 4)) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = expression(FEV[1] ~ (L)),     title = "FEV\u2081 Temporal Trend"   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 9, y = 3.5,     label = "68% CI band",     hjust = 1, size = 3   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.np.afib.mult.avrg_curv.binary.sas, tp.np.afib.mult.pt_spec.binary.sas, tp.np.avpkgrad_ozak_ind_mtwt.sas, tp.np.fev.multivariate.continuous.sas Provide named vector groups generating sample data, set group_col supplying curve data.
code: grp_def <- c("Ozaki" = 0.7, "CE-Pericardial" = 1.1, "Homograft" = 1.4) dat_grp     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n        = 600,   time_max = 12,   groups   = grp_def ) dat_grp_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(   n        = 600,   time_max = 12,   groups   = grp_def )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_grp,   group_col   = "group",   lower_col   = "lower",   upper_col   = "upper",   data_points = dat_grp_pts ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "#003087", "CE-Pericardial" = "#CC0000", "Homograft" = "#666666")   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Ozaki" = "#003087", "CE-Pericardial" = "#CC0000", "Homograft" = "#666666")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Prevalence (%)",     colour = "Valve type",     fill   = "Valve type",     title  = "Atrial Fibrillation by Valve Type"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.np.afib.ivwristm.pt_spec_phases.binary.sas, tp.np.tr.ivecho.u.phases.sas Phase plots separate early (bell-shaped, incomplete hazard) late (Weibull CDF, complete hazard) components. Supply group_col whose levels label phases.
code: dat_phase <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(   n      = 500,   groups = c("Early phase" = 1.5, "Late phase" = 0.6, "Overall" = 1.0) )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   dat_phase,   group_col = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Early phase" = "#CC0000",                "Late phase"  = "#003087",                "Overall"     = "black")   ) +   scale_linetype_manual(     values = c("Early phase" = "dashed",                "Late phase"  = "dashed",                "Overall"     = "solid")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Prevalence (%)",     colour = NULL,     title  = "AF — Early and Late Phase Decomposition"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Port: tp.np.z0axdpo.continuous.bmi_xaxis.sas BMI another continuous covariate (rather time) x-axis, function signature identical — simply pass covariate column name x_col.
code: # Simulate covariate (BMI) x-axis data set.seed(42) n_pts  <- 300 bmi    <- seq(18, 45, length.out = n_pts) est    <- plogis(-3 + 0.08 * bmi) se     <- sqrt(est * (1 - est) / 50) bmi_curve <- data.frame(   bmi   = bmi,   est   = est,   lower = pmax(0, est - qnorm(0.84) * se),   upper = pmin(1, est + qnorm(0.84) * se) )  nonparametric_curve_plot(   bmi_curve,   x_col        = "bmi",   estimate_col = "est",   lower_col    = "lower",   upper_col    = "upper" ) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = seq(18, 45, 3),     limits = c(18, 45)   ) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = expression(BMI ~ (kg/m^2)),     y     = "Estimated Probability",     title = "Outcome Probability vs. BMI at Operation"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

SAS reshape (reading R): R reshape:  Port: tp.np.po_ar.u_multi.ordinal.sas  Port: tp.np.tr.ivecho.independence.sas examine single grade isolation, filter long-format curve passing function:  Port: tp.np.tr.ivecho.u.phases.sas Phase-decomposed ordinal plots combine phase labels grade colours. code: /* SAS wide format: p0, p1, p2, p3 */ data predict_wide;   set predict; run; library(tidyr) long <- pivot_longer(   predict_wide,   cols      = c(p0, p1, p2, p3),   names_to  = "grade",   values_to = "estimate" ) ord_labels <- c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe") dat_ord     <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(   n            = 1000,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = ord_labels ) dat_ord_pts <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(   n            = 1000,   time_max     = 5,   grade_labels = ord_labels )  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(   dat_ord,   grade_col   = "grade",   data_points = dat_ord_pts ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(       "None"     = "#003087",       "Mild"     = "#55A51C",       "Moderate" = "#FFA500",       "Severe"   = "#CC0000"     )   ) + = "Grade probability",     colour = "TR Grade",     title  = "Tricuspid Regurgitation Grade"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ar1 <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(seed = 1) dat_ar2 <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(seed = 99)  dat_ar1$scenario <- "Before repair" dat_ar2$scenario <- "After repair" combined <- rbind(dat_ar1, dat_ar2)  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(combined, grade_col = "grade") +   facet_wrap(~scenario) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x      = "Years",     y      = "Grade probability",     colour = "AR Grade",     title  = "AR Grade — Before vs. After Repair"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ind    <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(n = 800) pts_ind    <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(n = 800) grade_2    <- dat_ind[dat_ind$grade == "Grade 2", ] dp_grade_2 <- pts_ind[pts_ind$grade == "Grade 2", ]  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(   grade_2,   grade_col   = "grade",   data_points = dp_grade_2 ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Grade 2" = "#CC0000")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 0.5),     labels = scales::percent   ) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Probability",     title = "Probability of TR Grade 2"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_ph <- sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(n = 800, seed = 7)  nonparametric_ordinal_plot(dat_ph, grade_col = "grade") +   annotate("rect",     xmin = 0, xmax = 2,  ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,     fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.07   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 1, y = 0.95, label = "Early\nphase",     size = 3, colour = "steelblue", fontface = "italic"   ) +   annotate("rect",     xmin = 2, xmax = 5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,     fill = "tomato", alpha = 0.07   ) +   annotate("text",     x = 3.5, y = 0.95, label = "Late\nphase",     size = 3, colour = "tomato", fontface = "italic"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +   labs(     x      = "Years after operation",     y      = "Grade probability",     colour = "TR Grade",     title  = "TR Grade — Early and Late Phase"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.ac.dead.sas, tp.cp.dead.sas R equivalent: survival_curve() survival_curve() wraps survfit() survival package returns single bare ggplot selected plot_type (default "survival"). five plots matching SAS %kaplan / %nelsont macro output flags (PLOTS, PLOTC, PLOTH, PLOTL) plus tidy data frames attached attributes (e.g., attr(result, "km_data")).
code: dta    <- sample_survival_data(n = 500, seed = 42) result <- survival_curve(dta)  # Kaplan–Meier survival (PLOTS) — result IS the bare ggplot result +   scale_y_continuous(     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(     x     = "Years after operation",     y     = "Survival (%)",     title = "Freedom from Death"   ) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Cumulative hazard (PLOTC) survival_curve(dta, plot_type = "cumhaz") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Cumulative Hazard") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Hazard rate (PLOTH) survival_curve(dta, plot_type = "hazard") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Instantaneous Hazard") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Log-log survival (PH check) survival_curve(dta, plot_type = "loglog") +   labs(x = "log(Years)", y = "log(-log S(t))") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Integrated survivorship (PLOTL) survival_curve(dta, plot_type = "life") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Restricted Mean Survival (years)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Access the KM data, risk table, and report table via attr() attr(result, "km_data") attr(result, "risk_table") attr(result, "report_table")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Port: tp.lp.propen.cov_balance.R R equivalent: covariate_balance() SAS export arrives wide (one column per time-point). Reshape long format calling covariate_balance(). Pass var_levels control bottom--top display order covariates (matches ylabel original script).
code: # Reshape wide → long (mirrors the original script) ylabel <- c(   "Cardiac Output", "BAV", "Cardiac Index", "NYHA -Functional Class",   "COPD-Oxy", "Date of Surg", "Creatinine", "Age", "CAD",   "Female", "LV PWT", "Mismatch", "HTN", "PVD", "Sinitibular Jcn: diam" )  names(dta) <- c("variable", "Before match", "After match")  dta_long <- reshape(   dta,   direction = "long",   varying   = c("Before match", "After match"),   v.names   = "std_diff",   timevar   = "group",   times     = c("Before match", "After match"),   idvar     = "variable" )  n_vars <- length(ylabel)  stdifPlot <- covariate_balance(   data         = dta_long,   variable_col = "variable",   group_col    = "group",   std_diff_col = "std_diff",   var_levels   = ylabel ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-40, 30), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference: SAVR - TF:TAVR (%)", y = "") +   annotate("text", x = -30, y = 0,      label = "More likely TF-TAVR", size = 4.5) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = n_vars, label = "More likely SAVR",    size = 4.5) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.935)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(here::here("graphs", "lp_cov-balance-SAVR_TF-TAVR.pdf"),        plot = stdifPlot, height = 7, width = 8)

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Source: PAM clustering analysis pipeline (R script using ggsankey) R equivalent: cluster_sankey_plot() cluster_sankey_plot() draws Sankey diagram showing patients flow letter-labelled clusters number clusters K increases 2 9. column one K; flow bands show assignment changes consecutive K values; node labels show cluster letter + patient count. original code used ggsankey::make_long() hard-coded column names ordering vectors. R port accepts cluster columns ordering via cluster_cols node_levels arguments. Requires ggsankey (install GitHub): reproduce original analysis patient-level cluster assignments:
code: remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey") dta_san <- sample_cluster_sankey_data(n = 300, seed = 42)  cluster_sankey_plot(dta_san) +   labs(title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 9") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") # Build a data frame with one row per patient, columns C2..C9 (factor) grp_dta <- data.frame(   C2 = factor(pm2clusters_labels, levels = gr2_names),   C3 = factor(pm3clusters_labels, levels = gr3_names),   # ... etc.   C9 = factor(pm9clusters_labels, levels = gr9_names) )  cluster_sankey_plot(   grp_dta,   cluster_cols = paste0("C", 2:9),   node_levels  = gr9_names ) +   labs(x = NULL) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

Template uses values=c("red", "blue"); modernised equivalents .
code: dta <- sample_spaghetti_data(n_patients = 150, max_obs = 6)  spaghetti_plot(dta) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 30, 10)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 30)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 5)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Area (EOA) (cm\u00b2)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") spaghetti_plot(dta, colour_col = "group") +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1.25, 0.25)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 1.25)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "DVI") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_ord       <- dta dta_ord$value <- round(pmin(3, pmax(0, dta$value / 12))) levels(dta_ord$group) <- c("Early", "Late")  spaghetti_plot(   dta_ord,   colour_col = "group",   y_labels   = c(None = 0, Mild = 1, Moderate = 2, Severe = 3) ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(Early = "steelblue", Late = "red2"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 6, 1)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6), ylim = c(0, 3)) +   labs(x = "Years after Procedure", y = "MV Regurgitation Grade") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")
