text: Ports: tp.rp.trends.sas, tp.lp.trends.sas, tp.lp.trends.age.sas, tp.lp.trends.polytomous.sas, tp.dp.trends.R R equivalent: trends_plot() Multiple outcomes one figure. CGM version: axisx order=(1970 2000 10), axisy order=(0 100 20).
code: one <- sample_trends_data(n = 600, year_range = c(1968L, 2000L), groups = NULL)  # Cases/year: axisy order=(0 to 10 by 2) trends_plot(one, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1968, 2000), breaks = seq(1968, 2000, 4)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10),      breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Cases/year") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  # Age: axisy order=(30 to 70 by 10) trends_plot(one, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1968, 2000), breaks = seq(1968, 2000, 4)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(30, 70),     breaks = seq(30, 70, 10)) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Age (years)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_lp <- sample_trends_data(   n = 800, year_range = c(1970L, 2000L),   groups = c("Shock %", "Pre-op IABP %", "Inotropes %"))  trends_plot(dta_lp) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Shock %" = "steelblue", "Pre-op IABP %" = "firebrick",                "Inotropes %" = "forestgreen"), name = NULL) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Shock %" = 16L, "Pre-op IABP %" = 15L, "Inotropes %" = 17L),     name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1970, 2000), breaks = seq(1970, 2000, 10)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1970, 2000), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_age <- sample_trends_data(   n = 600, year_range = c(25L, 85L),   groups = c("Repair %", "Bioprosthesis %"), seed = 7L)  trends_plot(dta_age) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Repair %" = "steelblue", "Bioprosthesis %" = "firebrick"),     name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 85), breaks = seq(25, 85, 10)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(25, 85), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Percent (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_poly <- sample_trends_data(   n = 800, year_range = c(1990L, 1999L),   groups = c("CE", "Cosgrove", "Periguard", "DeVega"), seed = 5L)  trends_plot(dta_poly) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(CE = "steelblue", Cosgrove = "firebrick",                Periguard = "forestgreen", DeVega = "goldenrod3"),     name = "Repair type") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c(CE = 15L, Cosgrove = 19L, Periguard = 17L, DeVega = 18L),     name = "Repair type") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1990, 1999), breaks = seq(1990, 1999, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1990, 1999), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_lv <- sample_trends_data(n = 800, year_range = c(1995L, 2015L),                               groups = NULL, seed = 3L)  trends_plot(dta_lv, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1995, 2015), breaks = seq(1995, 2015, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 200),     breaks = seq(0, 200, 50)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1995, 2015), ylim = c(0, 200)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "LV Mass Index") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta_los <- sample_trends_data(n = 800, year_range = c(1985L, 2015L),                                groups = NULL, seed = 11L)  trends_plot(dta_los, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1985, 2015), breaks = seq(1985, 2015, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20),      breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1985, 2015), ylim = c(0, 20)) +   annotate("text", x = 1995, y = 18,            label = "Trend: Hospital Length of Stay", size = 4.5) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Hospital LOS (Days)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
code: dta_age <- sample_trends_data(   n = 600, year_range = c(25L, 85L),   groups = c("Repair %", "Bioprosthesis %"), seed = 7L)  trends_plot(dta_age) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Repair %" = "steelblue", "Bioprosthesis %" = "firebrick"),     name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 85), breaks = seq(25, 85, 10)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(25, 85), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Percent (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

what: tp.lp.trends.polytomous.sas — repair types (1990–1999 by 1, y 0–100 by 10)
title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
code: dta_poly <- sample_trends_data(   n = 800, year_range = c(1990L, 1999L),   groups = c("CE", "Cosgrove", "Periguard", "DeVega"), seed = 5L)  trends_plot(dta_poly) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(CE = "steelblue", Cosgrove = "firebrick",                Periguard = "forestgreen", DeVega = "goldenrod3"),     name = "Repair type") +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c(CE = 15L, Cosgrove = 19L, Periguard = 17L, DeVega = 18L),     name = "Repair type") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1990, 1999), breaks = seq(1990, 1999, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),     breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1990, 1999), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

what: tp.dp.trends.R — LV mass index (1995–2015 by 5, y 0–200 by 50)
title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
code: dta_lv <- sample_trends_data(n = 800, year_range = c(1995L, 2015L),                               groups = NULL, seed = 3L)  trends_plot(dta_lv, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1995, 2015), breaks = seq(1995, 2015, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 200),     breaks = seq(0, 200, 50)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1995, 2015), ylim = c(0, 200)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "LV Mass Index") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

what: tp.dp.trends.R — hospital LOS with annotation (1985–2015 by 5, y 0–20 by 5)
title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
code: dta_los <- sample_trends_data(n = 800, year_range = c(1985L, 2015L),                                groups = NULL, seed = 11L)  trends_plot(dta_los, group_col = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1985, 2015), breaks = seq(1985, 2015, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20),      breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1985, 2015), ylim = c(0, 20)) +   annotate("text", x = 1995, y = 18,            label = "Trend: Hospital Length of Stay", size = 4.5) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Hospital LOS (Days)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: R equivalents: tp.lp.mirror-histogram_SAVR-TF-TAVR.R (binary-match), tp.lp.mirror_histo_before_after_wt.R (weighted IPTW) scripts superseded mirror_histogram(). function returns bare ggplot object directly. Diagnostics printed message attached attr(mhist, "diagnostics"); working data attr(mhist, "data"). Compose scales, annotations, theme usual + operator.
code: dta   <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 400, separation = 1.5) mhist <- mirror_histogram(dta)   # defaults: prob_t / tavr / match  mhist +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0  = "white",  matched_g0 = "green1",       before_g1  = "white",  matched_g1 = "green4"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) +   annotate("text", x = 20, y =  100, label = "SAVR",    size = 7) +   annotate("text", x = 20, y = -100, label = "TF-TAVR", size = 7) +   labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "Number of Patients") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dta      <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 400, add_weights = TRUE) mhist_wt <- mirror_histogram(   dta,   group_labels = c("Limited", "Extended"),   weight_col   = "mt_wt" )  mhist_wt +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       before_g0   = "white", weighted_g0 = "blue",       before_g1   = "white", weighted_g1 = "red"     ),     guide = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) +   annotate("text", x = 30, y =  150, label = "Limited",  color = "blue", size = 5) +   annotate("text", x = 30, y = -70,  label = "Extended", color = "red",  size = 5) +   labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "#") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: plot functions return unstyled ggplot object. Add theme final layer: can pass base_size theme scale text simultaneously:
code: p + hvti_theme("manuscript", base_size = 10)   # smaller text for double-column p + hvti_theme("poster",     base_size = 24)   # larger text for A0 poster

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: save_ppt() inserts ggplot objects PowerPoint file editable DrawingML vector graphics via officer rvg packages — shapes, lines, text remain individually selectable PowerPoint export. first argument object (plot); output path powerpoint (file filename).
code: # Locate the bundled Cleveland Clinic slide template template <- system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR")  # Single slide — apply a PPT theme before saving p_ppt <- p +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Prevalence (%)") +   hvti_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = p_ppt,   template     = template,   powerpoint   = "figures/afib_prevalence.pptx",   slide_titles = "AF Prevalence over Time" )  # Multiple plots — one slide per figure in a single call save_ppt(   object       = list(fig1 = p_binary, fig2 = p_multi),   template     = template,   powerpoint   = "figures/np_curves.pptx",   slide_titles = c("Binary Outcome", "Multi-group Comparison") ) ggsave("figures/afib_prevalence.pdf",  p, width = 3.5, height = 3.5, units = "in") ggsave("figures/afib_prevalence.tiff", p, width = 3.5, height = 3.5,        units = "in", dpi = 600)

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.hs.dead.setup.sas, tp.hs.dead_uses_setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas, tp.hs.dead.conditional.setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.conditional.uses_setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.compare_benefit.setup.sas, tp.hs.uslife_estimates_generate_stratify_.age.sas, tp.hs.uslife_generates_matched_estimates.sas R equivalents: hazard_plot(), survival_difference_plot() tp.hs.* family extends tp.hp.dead.* using %hazpred macro generate patient-specific parametric survival predictions fitted multivariable hazard model. setup templates compute store: Cumulative hazard per patient last follow-(observed vs. expected goodness--fit tests via %chisqgf). Individual survivorship curves fine time grid (mean-curve aggregation PROC SUMMARY). Subsequent use templates stratify aggregated mean curves covariate overlay Kaplan-Meier estimates %kaplan. resulting SAS datasets map directly hazard_plot() R: Reproduces core figure tp.hs.dead_uses_setup.sas tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas: mean parametric survival curve (solid line confidence band) overlaid Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates (symbols error bars). tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas overlays curves depth--invasion groups (tdepth = 1, 2, 3). Pass group_col hazard_plot() supply matched sample_hazard_data() / sample_hazard_empirical() calls groups vector whose names become legend labels. tp.hs.dead.conditional.setup.sas computes individual survivorship curves starting time hospital discharge rather operation date. conditional survival S(t | discharge) S(t) / S(t_discharge) patient. R, hazard_plot() call used; x-axis label data preparation differ. tp.hs.uslife_* templates call %usmatchd generate age-, sex-, race-matched US population life-table survival curves patient. sample_life_table() provides representative matched curves stratified age group use R examples. tp.hs.dead.compare_benefit.setup.sas computes, patient, difference predicted survival fixed time point (5 years) two treatment arms (e.g. ASA vs. ASA). distribution individual differences shows benefits much. Use survival_difference_plot() plot mean curve time.
code: dat_hp <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_hp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_hp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_strat <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 10,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) ) emp_strat <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) )  hazard_plot(   dat_strat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_strat,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_cond <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_cond <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_cond,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_cond,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Discharge", y = "Survival after Discharge (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") dat_age <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 10,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) emp_age <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) lt <- sample_life_table(   age_groups = c("<60", "60\u201385", "\u226585"),   age_mids   = c(50, 72, 88),   time_max   = 10 )  hazard_plot(   dat_age,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp_age,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Age group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") diff_dat <- sample_survival_difference_data(   n      = 500,   groups = c("No ASA" = 1.0, "ASA" = 0.75) )  survival_difference_plot(   diff_dat,   lower_col = "diff_lower",   upper_col = "diff_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",                       colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-5, 40),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

text: tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas overlays curves depth--invasion groups (tdepth = 1, 2, 3). Pass group_col hazard_plot() supply matched sample_hazard_data() / sample_hazard_empirical() calls groups vector whose names become legend labels.
code: dat_strat <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 10,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) ) emp_strat <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) )  hazard_plot(   dat_strat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_strat,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: tp.hs.uslife_* templates call %usmatchd generate age-, sex-, race-matched US population life-table survival curves patient. sample_life_table() provides representative matched curves stratified age group use R examples.
code: dat_age <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 10,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) emp_age <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) lt <- sample_life_table(   age_groups = c("<60", "60\u201385", "\u226585"),   age_mids   = c(50, 72, 88),   time_max   = 10 )  hazard_plot(   dat_age,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp_age,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Age group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: ggplot2 themes plot functions creating publication-quality graphics R, conforming standards Cardiovascular Outcomes Registries Research (CORR) within Heart & Vascular Institute Cleveland Clinic. package modern R replacement historical plot.sas macro.

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: Install GitHub using remotes: Apply HVI theme ggplot2 figure one line: Export result directly editable PowerPoint slide:
code: remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR") library(ggplot2) library(hvtiPlotR)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + hvti_theme("ppt")  save_ppt(   object     = p,   template   = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),   powerpoint = "outputs/figure1.pptx" )

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: Use hvti_theme() apply supported style, call named aliases directly:
code: p + hvti_theme("manuscript")   # via generic p + theme_man()                # direct alias p + hvti_theme("ppt")          # dark PPT p + hvti_theme("light_ppt")    # light PPT p + hvti_theme("poster")       # poster

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
text: Produces Sankey (alluvial) diagram using ggalluvial::geom_alluvium() ggalluvial::geom_stratum(). Axes specified character vector number stages hard-coded. Returns bare ggplot object composition scale_*, labs(), hvti_theme().

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
code: alluvial_plot(   data,   axes,   y_col = "freq",   fill_col = NULL,   axis_labels = NULL,   stratum_fill = "grey80",   stratum_width = 1/4,   flow_width = 1/6,   alpha = 0.8,   knot_pos = 0.4,   show_labels = TRUE )

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
text: data data frame wide alluvial format: one row per axis-value combination, numeric weight column, one column per axis. axes Character vector column names use axes, left--right display order. Minimum two columns. y_col Name numeric weight column (counts proportions). Default "freq". fill_col Name column map flow fill colour aesthetics, NULL single fill. Default NULL. axis_labels Character vector axis labels x-axis, length axes. Defaults axes (column names). stratum_fill Fill colour stratum bars. Default "grey80". stratum_width Width stratum bars fraction axis spacing. Default 1/4. flow_width Width alluvium flows. Default 1/6. alpha Transparency flows, [0, 1]. Default 0.8. knot_pos Curvature flow ribbons, [0, 1]. Default 0.4. show_labels Logical; whether label stratum value. Default TRUE.

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object. Compose scale_fill_*, scale_colour_*, labs(), annotate(), hvti_theme().

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
text: Data format: one row per unique combination axis values, numeric weight column (y_col). matches frequency / summary table format used SAS template (ardat percent y). Colours: flows unfilled default. Map column fill_col add scale_fill_manual() scale_fill_brewer() returned object. Stratum bars use stratum_fill (default "grey80") independent flow fill scale.

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — alluvial_plot
code: dta  <- sample_alluvial_data(n = 300, seed = 42) axes <- c("pre_ar", "procedure", "post_ar")  # --- Bare plot ----------------------------------------------------------- alluvial_plot(dta, axes = axes, y_col = "freq") #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes.   # --- Fill flows by pre-operative AR grade + manuscript theme ------------- alluvial_plot(dta, axes = axes, y_col = "freq", fill_col = "pre_ar") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     name = "Pre-op AR"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     breaks = 1:3,     labels = c("Pre-op AR", "Procedure", "Post-op AR"),     expand = c(0.05, 0.05)   ) +   ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)",                 title = "AV Regurgitation: Pre- to Post-operative") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Scale for x is already present. #> Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes.   # --- Fill flows by procedure with RColorBrewer palette ------------------- alluvial_plot(dta, axes = axes, y_col = "freq", fill_col = "procedure") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Procedure") +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     breaks = 1:3,     labels = c("Pre-op AR", "Procedure", "Post-op AR"),     expand = c(0.05, 0.05)   ) +   ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Scale for x is already present. #> Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes.   # --- Two-axis (before / after) with annotation --------------------------- alluvial_plot(   dta, axes = c("pre_ar", "post_ar"), y_col = "freq",   fill_col = "pre_ar", axis_labels = c("Pre-operative", "Post-operative") ) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,                              name = "AR Grade") +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,                                guide = "none") +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = 250,                     label = "Improvement after surgery",                     size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +   ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)",                 title = "AV Regurgitation Before and After Surgery") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes.   # --- Save ---------------------------------------------------------------- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- alluvial_plot(dta, axes = axes, y_col = "freq", fill_col = "pre_ar") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,                                guide = "none") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") ggplot2::ggsave("sankey.pdf", p, width = 8, height = 6) } # }

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
text: Draws Sankey diagram showing patients flow labelled clusters number clusters K increases. column represents one value K; band shows fraction patients whose cluster assignment changes K K+1. Node labels show cluster letter patient count.

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
code: cluster_sankey_plot(   data,   cluster_cols = paste0("C", 2:9),   node_levels = NULL,   node_colours = NULL,   alpha = 0.8,   label_size = 8,   label_hjust = -0.05 )

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
text: data Data frame; one row per patient. Must contain columns named cluster_cols. cluster_cols Character vector column names giving cluster assignments value K, ascending K order. Default paste0("C", 2:9) (columns C2, C3, …, C9). node_levels Character vector giving display order node labels (bottom top within column). NULL (default), existing factor levels first cluster column used; column factor, levels taken order first appearance. node_colours Named character vector mapping node labels fill colours. NULL (default), colours drawn RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1") order c(2, 6, 8, 4, 3, 5, 7, 1, 9) matching original analysis. Truncated extended automatically number unique nodes. alpha Transparency applied flow bands node labels. Default 0.8. label_size Font size node labels points. Default 8. label_hjust Horizontal justification offset node labels. Negative values shift labels right node centre. Default -0.05.

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object using ggsankey geoms. Compose scale_fill_manual(), labs(), theme(), hvti_theme().

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
text: ports PAM cluster stability figure produced HVTI clustering analysis pipeline (source code using ggsankey::make_long(), geom_sankey(), geom_sankey_label()). Requires ggsankey package. Install :
code: remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey")

title: Cluster Stability Sankey Plot — cluster_sankey_plot
code: dta <- sample_cluster_sankey_data(n = 300, seed = 42) head(dta) #>   C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9 #> 1  B  B  B  B  F  F  H  H #> 2  B  B  B  B  F  F  H  H #> 3  A  A  A  A  A  A  A  A #> 4  A  A  A  A  A  G  G  G #> 5  B  B  D  D  D  D  D  D #> 6  B  B  B  B  F  F  F  F table(dta$C9) #>  #>  B  F  H  D  I  C  E  G  A  #> 59 39 22 26 11 42 38 22 41   # cluster_sankey_plot() requires ggsankey (GitHub-only package). # Install with: remotes::install_github("davidsjoberg/ggsankey") if (requireNamespace("ggsankey", quietly = TRUE)) {    # --- Default colours (Set1 palette) -----------------------------------   cluster_sankey_plot(dta) +     ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 9") +     hvti_theme("manuscript")    # --- Custom colour palette --------------------------------------------   my_cols <- c(     A = "#1f77b4", B = "#ff7f0e", C = "#2ca02c", D = "#d62728",     E = "#9467bd", F = "#8c564b", G = "#e377c2", H = "#7f7f7f",     I = "#bcbd22"   )   cluster_sankey_plot(dta, node_colours = my_cols) +     ggplot2::labs(x = NULL) +     hvti_theme("manuscript")    # --- Subset to K = 2 to 6 only ----------------------------------------   cluster_sankey_plot(dta, cluster_cols = paste0("C", 2:6)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = NULL) +     hvti_theme("manuscript") } #> Warning: The `size` argument of `element_rect()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggsankey package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/davidsjoberg/ggsankey/issues>.

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
text: Draws classic HVI covariate balance figure used assess propensity score matching IPTW quality. covariate appears labelled row; points display standardized mean difference group (e.g. matching). solid reference line marks zero; dotted reference lines mark user-supplied imbalance threshold (default +/-10\

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
code: covariate_balance(   data,   variable_col = "variable",   group_col = "group",   std_diff_col = "std_diff",   var_levels = NULL,   threshold = 10,   point_size = 3,   alpha = 0.8,   hline_linetype = "dashed",   hline_linewidth = 0.25,   vline_linewidth = 0.2,   threshold_linetype = "dotted" )

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
text: data data frame long format one row per covariate x group combination. Wide-format data (one column per group) must reshaped passing, e.g. tidyr::pivot_longer() stats::reshape(). variable_col Name column containing covariate labels. Default "variable". group_col Name column identifying comparison group (e.g. "match" / "match"). Default "group". std_diff_col Name numeric column holding standardized mean difference values. Default "std_diff". var_levels Character vector controlling display order covariates y-axis. first element appears bottom. Defaults order first appearance data[[variable_col]]. threshold Numeric value (absolute) dotted imbalance reference lines drawn +/-threshold. Default 10. point_size Passed ggplot2::geom_point(). Default 3. alpha Transparency point glyphs, [0, 1]. Default 0.8. hline_linetype Linetype horizontal covariate guide lines. Default "dashed". hline_linewidth Linewidth horizontal guide lines. Default 0.25. vline_linewidth Linewidth solid zero reference line. Default 0.2. threshold_linetype Linetype +/-threshold reference lines. Default "dotted".

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
text: bare ggplot2::ggplot() object. Layer ggplot2::scale_color_manual(), ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(), ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(), ggplot2::labs(), ggplot2::annotate(), theme complete figure.

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
text: function returns bare ggplot object colour, shape, axis, theme applied. Callers expected add usual + operator, keeping workflow flexible consistent rest package.

title: Covariate Balance Plot — covariate_balance
code: library(ggplot2)  dta <- sample_covariate_balance_data()  # Bare plot with manuscript theme covariate_balance(dta, alpha = 0.8) +   hvtiPlotR::hvti_theme("manuscript")   # Add colour, shape, axis scales, and manuscript theme covariate_balance(dta, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-45, 35), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(     x = "Standardized difference: Group A vs Group B (%)",     y = ""   ) +   hvtiPlotR::hvti_theme("manuscript") +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.95)) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # Add directional annotations and theme covariate_balance(dta, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-45, 35), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference (%)", y = "") +   annotate("text", x = -32, y =  0.5,            label = "More likely Group B", size = 4) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = 13.5,            label = "More likely Group A", size = 4) +   hvtiPlotR::hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

code: eda_classify_var(x, unique_limit = 6L)

code: eda_classify_var(c(0, 1, 1, 0, NA))        # "Cat_Num" #> [1] "Cat_Num" eda_classify_var(c(1, 2, 3, 4))            # "Cat_Num" #> [1] "Cat_Num" eda_classify_var(rnorm(50))                # "Cont" #> [1] "Cont" eda_classify_var(c("A", "B", "A"))         # "Cat_Char" #> [1] "Cat_Char"

what: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
text: Produces exploratory data analysis plot single variable reference time axis. Variable type detected automatically using eda_classify_var():

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
code: eda_plot(   data,   x_col = "year",   y_col = "ef",   y_label = NULL,   unique_limit = 6L,   show_percent = FALSE,   smooth_method = "loess",   smooth_span = 0.8,   smooth_se = FALSE )

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
text: data Data frame; one row per observation. x_col Name reference (time/grouping) column. Used x-axis scatter bar plots. barplots coerced factor. Default "year". y_col Name variable plot. Default "ef". y_label Optional human-readable label variable, used plot title, y-axis label (continuous), fill-legend name (categorical). NULL (default), y_col used. Matches var_labels / var.names override tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R. unique_limit Integer threshold passed eda_classify_var() distinguish categorical continuous numeric columns. Default 6. show_percent Logical; categorical plots, use proportions (position = "fill") instead counts (position = "stack")? Default FALSE. smooth_method Smoothing method continuous plots, passed ggplot2::geom_smooth(). Default "loess". smooth_span LOESS span. Default 0.8. smooth_se Logical; show confidence ribbon around smooth? Default FALSE.

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
text: Continuous ("Cont"): scatter y_col vs x_col LOESS smoother overlay rug x-axis observations y_col missing. Numeric categorical ("Cat_Num"): stacked bar chart counts (proportions show_percent = TRUE) per x_col level. Character categorical ("Cat_Char"): stacked bar, colouring string level separately. cases NA values shown explicit fill level labelled "(Missing)", can coloured via scale_fill_manual(). Use eda_select_vars() pick named subset variables iterating lapply(). Returns bare ggplot2::ggplot() object. Compose scale_fill_*, scale_colour_*, labs(), annotate(), hvti_theme().

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
text: R templates: tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots.R, tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R; helper: Barplot_Scatterplot_Function.R (Function_DataPlotting(), Order_Variables()).

title: EDA Barplot / Scatterplot for One Variable — eda_plot
code: dta <- sample_eda_data(n = 300, seed = 42)  # --- Binary categorical: count barplot ------------------------------------ # male is 0/1; y_label sets title and fill legend name. eda_plot(dta, x_col = "year", y_col = "male",          y_label = "Sex") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c("0" = "steelblue", "1" = "firebrick", "(Missing)" = "grey80"),     labels = c("0" = "Female", "1" = "Male", "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- Binary categorical: percentage barplot ------------------------------- eda_plot(dta, x_col = "year", y_col = "cabg",          y_label = "Concomitant CABG", show_percent = TRUE) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c("0" = "grey70", "1" = "steelblue", "(Missing)" = "grey90"),     labels = c("0" = "No CABG", "1" = "CABG", "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   # --- Ordinal categorical (4 levels) with RColorBrewer -------------------- eda_plot(dta, x_col = "year", y_col = "nyha",          y_label = "Preoperative NYHA Class") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(     palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1,     labels  = c("1" = "I", "2" = "II", "3" = "III", "4" = "IV",                 "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name    = "NYHA"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- Character categorical ----------------------------------------------- eda_plot(dta, x_col = "year", y_col = "valve_morph",          y_label = "Valve Morphology") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(Bicuspid  = "steelblue",                Tricuspid = "firebrick",                Unicuspid = "goldenrod3",                "(Missing)" = "grey80"),     name = "Morphology"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- Continuous: scatter + LOESS ----------------------------------------- eda_plot(dta, x_col = "op_years", y_col = "ef",          y_label = "Ejection Fraction (%)") +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(20, 80),                               breaks = seq(20, 80, 20)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year",                 caption = "Tick marks: observations with missing EF") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- Continuous: annotated ----------------------------------------------- eda_plot(dta, x_col = "op_years", y_col = "peak_grad",          y_label = "Peak Gradient (mmHg)") +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +   ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 12, y = 70,                     label = "LOESS span = 0.8",                     size = 3, colour = "grey40", fontface = "italic") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- Variable selection + labels (varnames template pattern) -------------- # Matches Var_CatList / var_labels workflow in # tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R. # Named vector: names = column names, values = human-readable labels. bin_vars <- c(male = "Sex (Male)", cabg = "Concomitant CABG") sub_bin  <- eda_select_vars(dta, c("year", names(bin_vars))) p_bin <- lapply(names(bin_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_bin, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = bin_vars[[cn]]) +     ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", direction = -1,                                name = NULL) +     ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_bin[[1]]  p_bin[[2]]   # --- Variable selection: ordinal / multi-level categorical ---------------- # Matches Var_CatList with Min_Categories=3, Max_Categories=7. cat_vars <- c(nyha        = "NYHA Class",               valve_morph = "Valve Morphology") sub_cat <- eda_select_vars(dta, c("year", names(cat_vars))) p_cat <- lapply(names(cat_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cat, x_col = "year", y_col = cn,            y_label = cat_vars[[cn]]) +     ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = NULL) +     ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cat[[1]]   # --- Variable selection: continuous --------------------------------------- # Matches Var_ContList / var_labels workflow. cont_vars <- c(ef        = "Ejection Fraction (%)",                lv_mass   = "LV Mass Index (g/m\u00b2)",                peak_grad = "Peak Gradient (mmHg)") sub_cont <- eda_select_vars(dta, c("op_years", names(cont_vars))) p_cont <- lapply(names(cont_vars), function(cn) {   eda_plot(sub_cont, x_col = "op_years", y_col = cn,            y_label = cont_vars[[cn]]) +     ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +     ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +     hvti_theme("manuscript") }) p_cont[[1]]   # --- Save: multi-page PDF via ggsave + gridExtra ------------------------- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ all_plots <- c(p_bin, p_cat, p_cont) per_page  <- 9L  # 3 x 3 grid for (pg in seq(1, length(all_plots), by = per_page)) {   idx  <- seq(pg, min(pg + per_page - 1L, length(all_plots)))   grob <- gridExtra::marrangeGrob(all_plots[idx], nrow = 3, ncol = 3)   ggplot2::ggsave(     filename = sprintf("eda_page%02d.pdf", ceiling(pg / per_page)),     plot     = grob,     width    = 14, height = 14   ) } } # }

what: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
text: Returns subset data containing columns named vars, order given. Replaces Order_Variables() helper Mod_Data <- dta[, Order_Var] pattern tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R.

title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
code: eda_select_vars(data, vars)

title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
text: data data frame. vars character vector column names, single string space-separated names, e.g. "age ht wt bmi".

title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
text: data frame containing requested columns requested order.

title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
text: vars may supplied character vector single space-separated string (matching Var_CatList / Var_ContList style template).

title: Select and Reorder Variables from a Data Frame — eda_select_vars
code: dta <- sample_eda_data()  # Vector form sub <- eda_select_vars(dta, c("male", "cabg", "nyha")) names(sub) #> [1] "male" "cabg" "nyha"  # Space-separated string (matches template Var_CatList style) sub2 <- eda_select_vars(dta, "male cabg nyha valve_morph") names(sub2) #> [1] "male"        "cabg"        "nyha"        "valve_morph"

what: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
title: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
text: Produces event follow-panel: patient plotted operation date (x) follow-time (y), three-level state factor driving colour shape: event-free, non-fatal event, death. Compose scale_color_manual(), scale_shape_manual(), labs(), hvti_theme().

title: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
code: goodness_event_plot(   data,   event_col,   event_time_col,   death_for_event_col = NULL,   event_levels = c("No event", "Non-fatal event", "Death"),   iv_opyrs_col = "iv_opyrs",   death_col = "dead",   origin_year = 1990,   study_start = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   tolower_names = TRUE,   alpha = 0.8,   segment_drop = 0.2,   diagonal_color = "orange",   diagonal_linetype = "dashed",   diagonal_linewidth = 0.6 )

title: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
text: data Data frame. See sample_goodness_followup_data(). event_col Name non-fatal event indicator column (required, default). event_time_col Name time--event column (required, default). death_for_event_col Name death column use event panel, NULL use default death_col. Default NULL. event_levels Character vector exactly three labels: event-free, non-fatal event, death. Default c("event", "Non-fatal event", "Death"). iv_opyrs_col Column name holding numeric interval (years) origin_year operation date. death_col Logical (coercible) indicator death. Used death panel , default, death component event panel. origin_year Reference calendar year matches zero iv_opyrs_col. study_start, study_end, close_date Dates define diagonal potential follow-line. tolower_names TRUE, column names converted lower case prior processing. alpha Transparency passed point segment layers. segment_drop Amount (years) subtracted follow-value draw short vertical tick beneath point. diagonal_color Color potential follow-reference line. Default "orange". diagonal_linetype Line type reference line. Default "dashed". diagonal_linewidth Line width reference line. Default 0.6.

title: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object (event panel).

title: Goodness of Follow-Up — Event Panel — goodness_event_plot
code: dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data()  goodness_event_plot(   dta,   event_col      = "ev_event",   event_time_col = "iv_event" ) +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c("No event" = "blue", "Non-fatal event" = "green3",                "Death" = "red"),     name = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 2, 4), name = NULL) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
text: Converts raw follow-extracts (either -memory data frames SAS transport files) tidy frames, draws classic HVI goodness follow-death panel. non-fatal event panel use goodness_event_plot().

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
code: goodness_followup(   data,   iv_opyrs_col = "iv_opyrs",   death_col = "dead",   death_time_col = "iv_dead",   origin_year = 1990,   study_start = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   tolower_names = TRUE,   death_levels = c("Alive", "Dead"),   alpha = 0.8,   segment_drop = 0.2,   diagonal_color = "orange",   diagonal_linetype = "dashed",   diagonal_linewidth = 0.6 )

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
text: data Data frame path SAS transport (.xpt) file containing follow-data. iv_opyrs_col Column name holding numeric interval (years) origin_year operation date. death_col Logical (coercible) indicator death. Used death panel , default, death component event panel. death_time_col Column containing follow-time death (censoring) expressed years. origin_year Reference calendar year matches zero iv_opyrs_col. study_start, study_end, close_date Dates define diagonal potential follow-line. tolower_names TRUE, column names converted lower case prior processing. death_levels Length-2 character vector naming "alive" "dead" states death panel. Default c("Alive", "Dead"). alpha Transparency passed point segment layers. segment_drop Amount (years) subtracted follow-value draw short vertical tick beneath point. diagonal_color Color potential follow-reference line. Default "orange". diagonal_linetype Line type reference line. Default "dashed". diagonal_linewidth Line width reference line. Default 0.6.

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object (death panel). Compose scale_color_manual(), scale_shape_manual(), labs(), hvti_theme(). event panel use goodness_event_plot().

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
text: function focuses preparing data, mapping states aesthetics, drawing scaffolding geoms; callers expected finish styling via standard ggplot2 modifiers (scale_*(), labs(), theme_*()), keeping plotting workflow flexible.

title: Build goodness-of-follow-up plots — goodness_followup
code: dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data()  goodness_followup(dta) +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c("Alive" = "blue", "Dead" = "red"), name = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 4), name = NULL) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: Plots pre-computed parametric survival, hazard, cumulative-hazard curve Weibull (parametric) model, optionally overlaid Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates population life-table reference. Covers complete family tp.hp.dead.* SAS templates.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
code: hazard_plot(   curve_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL,   empirical = NULL,   emp_x_col = x_col,   emp_estimate_col = "estimate",   emp_lower_col = NULL,   emp_upper_col = NULL,   emp_group_col = group_col,   reference = NULL,   ref_x_col = x_col,   ref_estimate_col = estimate_col,   ref_group_col = NULL,   ci_alpha = 0.2,   line_width = 1,   point_size = 2,   point_shape = 1L,   errorbar_width = 0.25 )

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: curve_data Data frame parametric predictions (fine grid). Typical source: SAS predict dataset exported CSV, sample_hazard_data(). x_col Name time (age) column. Corresponds YEARS / iv_dead SAS. Default "time". estimate_col Name predicted-value column. Use "survival" survival plot, "hazard" hazard-rate plot, "cumhaz" cumulative-hazard plot. Corresponds SSURVIV, hazard, cumhaz SAS output. Default "survival". lower_col Name lower CI column curve_data, NULL ribbon. Corresponds SCLLSURV / cll_p95. Default NULL. upper_col Name upper CI column curve_data, NULL. Corresponds SCLUSURV / clu_p95. Default NULL. group_col Name stratification column curve_data, NULL single curve. Default NULL. empirical Optional data frame Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates (discrete time points). Corresponds SAS plout / acpdms dataset. See sample_hazard_empirical(). Default NULL. emp_x_col Column name x empirical. Default: x_col. emp_estimate_col Column name y empirical. Default: estimate_col. emp_lower_col Column name error-bar lower bound empirical, NULL error bars. Default NULL. emp_upper_col Column name error-bar upper bound empirical, NULL. Default NULL. emp_group_col Column name grouping empirical. Default: group_col. reference Optional data frame population life-table survival curves. Corresponds SAS smatched / hmatched datasets. See sample_life_table(). Rendered dashed lines. Default NULL. ref_x_col Column name x reference. Default: x_col. ref_estimate_col Column name y reference. Default: estimate_col. ref_group_col Column name used vary linetype reference curves (e.g. age group). Default NULL. ci_alpha Transparency parametric CI ribbon ([0, 1]). Default 0.20. line_width Width parametric curve line. SAS width=3 corresponds roughly 1.2. Default 1.0. point_size Size empirical overlay points. Default 2.0. point_shape Shape code empirical points. 1 = open circle (SAS symbol=circle); 0 = open square (SAS symbol=square). Default 1. errorbar_width Width error bars empirical points. Default 0.25.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: SAS column mapping: x_col          ← YEARS / iv_dead estimate_col   ← SSURVIV (survival), hazard (%/yr), cumhaz lower_col      ← SCLLSURV / cll_p95 upper_col      ← SCLUSURV / clu_p95 group_col      ← treatment/group indicator variable empirical      ← plout / acpdms KM output dataset reference      ← smatched life-table dataset Returns bare ggplot object; compose scale_colour_*, scale_y_continuous(), labs(), hvti_theme().

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: SAS templates: tp.hp.dead.sas, tp.hp.dead.tkdn.stratified.sas, tp.hp.dead.age_with_population_life_table.sas, tp.hp.dead.uslife.stratifed.sas, tp.hp.dead.matching_weight.sas, tp.hp.dead.limited_FET.mtwt.sas, tp.hp.event.weighted.sas, tp.hp.repeated.event.weighted.sas, tp.hp.repeated_events.sas, tp.hp.mcs.mod.dead.devseq_nlvlv12.scallop.sas.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
code: library(ggplot2)  dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  # --- (1) Basic survival curve + KM overlay ------------------------------- # Matches tp.hp.dead.sas (survival panel). # SAS: SSURVIV on y-axis, SCLLSURV/SCLUSURV for CI, plout circles + bars. hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (2) Hazard rate curve + KM overlay ---------------------------------- # Matches tp.hp.dead.sas (hazard panel). # SAS: hazard on y-axis (%/year). KM empirical hazard not shown here # (the empirical overlay in SAS is the survival-based dot plot; for hazard, # only the parametric curve is typically shown). hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col = "hazard",   lower_col    = "haz_lower",   upper_col    = "haz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%/yr")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Hazard (%/year)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") #> Warning: Removed 128 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).   # --- (3) Cumulative hazard (tp.hp.event.weighted.sas) -------------------- # For readmission / repeated-event analyses: cumhaz = -log(S)*100. # SAS: Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard. R: use cumhaz column from predict. hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col  = "cumhaz",   lower_col     = "cumhaz_lower",   upper_col     = "cumhaz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Cumulative Hazard (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (4) Stratified survival (tp.hp.dead.tkdn.stratified.sas) ------------ # Two groups (e.g. Takedown vs No Takedown) with different colours and # linetypes. KM empirical overlay uses matching colours. dat2 <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 400, time_max = 10,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) emp2 <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 400, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) hazard_plot(   dat2,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp2,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Esophagostomy", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (5) Life table overlay (tp.hp.dead.age_with_population_life_table) --- # Age-stratified study curves + US population life table reference (dashed). # SAS: smatched as dashed overlay; different symbols per age group. dat3 <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 12,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65-80" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) emp3 <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 12, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65-80" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) lt <- sample_life_table(time_max = 12)  hazard_plot(   dat3,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp3,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65-80" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     name = "Age Group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65-80" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (6) Multivariable risk profiles (tp.hp.dead.ideal_multivariable) ----- # Patient profiles with distinct covariate combinations (good vs poor risk). dat_risk <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 8,   groups = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)" = 0.4,              "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = 1.8) ) hazard_plot(   dat_risk,   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col    = "surv_lower",   upper_col    = "surv_upper",   group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)"              = "steelblue",                "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Patient profile"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)"              = "steelblue",                "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 8), breaks = 0:8) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Brain Metastases",        y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (7) Propensity-weighted / matched groups ----------------------------- # tp.hp.dead.matching_weight.sas / tp.hp.dead.limited_FET.mtwt.sas. # Propensity weighting is applied upstream (before plotting); the plot call # is identical to the basic stratified survival plot above. dat_match <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 300, time_max = 7,   groups = c("Limited FET" = 1.0, "Extended FET" = 0.72) ) emp_match <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 300, time_max = 7, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("Limited FET" = 1.0, "Extended FET" = 0.72) ) hazard_plot(   dat_match,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_match,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Limited FET" = "steelblue", "Extended FET" = "#8B4513"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Limited FET" = "steelblue", "Extended FET" = "#8B4513"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = 0:7) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Repair", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (8) Device sequencing (tp.hp.mcs.mod.dead.devseq) ------------------- # Survival conditioned on surviving the non-LVAD phase, then the LVAD phase. # In R: generate two separate prediction segments and rbind them. dat_dev1 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 200, time_max = 0.038,                                 shape = 0.5, scale = 0.5) dat_dev1$device <- "Non-LVAD (first 2 weeks)" dat_dev2 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 200, time_max = 0.25,                                 shape = 1.0, scale = 1.5) dat_dev2$time   <- dat_dev2$time + 0.038 dat_dev2$device <- "LVAD" dat_dev  <- rbind(dat_dev1, dat_dev2) hazard_plot(   dat_dev,   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "device" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Non-LVAD (first 2 weeks)" = "steelblue", "LVAD" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.29),                       breaks = c(0, 0.038, 0.08, 0.13, 0.19, 0.25),                       labels = c("0", "2w", "1m", "6w", "2m", "3m")) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),                       labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Time on Device", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")   # --- (9) Save (dontrun) -------------------------------------------------- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- hazard_plot(dat, estimate_col = "survival",                  lower_col = "surv_lower", upper_col = "surv_upper",                  empirical = emp,                  emp_lower_col = "lower", emp_upper_col = "upper") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") ggplot2::ggsave("survival.pdf", p, width = 11.5, height = 8) } # }

what: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: hvtiPlotR R port plot.sas macro suite used Clinical Investigations Statistics group within Heart & Vascular Institute Cleveland Clinic. produces publication-quality graphics conform HVI manuscript presentation standards using ggplot2 officer package. plot functions return bare ggplot2::ggplot() objects callers can apply additional ggplot2 layers, scales, themes without restriction. function ships sample_*() companion generates realistic synthetic data examples tests.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: Use hvti_theme() single entry point. Lower-level functions also exported direct use. hvti_theme(): Unified dispatcher — accepts "manuscript", "poster", "light_ppt", "dark_ppt". hvti_theme_manuscript() / theme_manuscript() / theme_man(): Clean white-background theme journal figures. hvti_theme_dark_ppt() / theme_dark_ppt() / theme_ppt() / hvti_theme_ppt(): Large-font theme dark panel PowerPoint slides. hvti_theme_light_ppt() / theme_light_ppt(): Large-font theme light panel background PowerPoint slides. hvti_theme_poster() / theme_poster(): Medium-font theme conference posters.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: save_ppt(): Insert one ggplot objects PowerPoint file editable DrawingML vector graphics via officer rvg. make_footnote() / makeFootnote(): Add draft footnote figure analysis; omit publication-ready output.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: mirror_histogram(): Side--side propensity-score histograms binary-matched IPTW-weighted cohorts. Ports tp.lp.mirror-histogram_* tp.lp.mirror_histo_before_after_wt SAS scripts. covariate_balance(): Standardised mean difference dot-plot propensity-score matching weighting. Ports tp.lp.propen.cov_balance.R. stacked_histogram(): Stacked filled histogram numeric variable group.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: survival_curve(): Kaplan-Meier Nelson-Aalen survival analysis returning five plot types (survival, cumulative hazard, hazard, log-log, life/RMST) plus risk report tables. Ports SAS %kaplan %nelsont macros tp.ac.dead.sas. hazard_plot(): Parametric hazard/survival curves pre-fitted model output, optional KM empirical overlay population life-table reference. Ports tp.hp.* template family. survival_difference_plot(): Mean survival difference (life gained) time two treatment arms, confidence ribbons. Ports tp.hp.dead.life-gained.sas. nnt_plot(): Number-needed--treat time derived survival difference. Ports tp.hp.numtreat.survdiff.matched.sas.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: nonparametric_curve_plot(): Two-phase nonparametric temporal trend curves binary continuous outcomes optional 68%/95% CI ribbon binned data summary points. Ports tp.np.* template family. nonparametric_ordinal_plot(): Grade-specific probability curves cumulative proportional-odds models (e.g. TR/AR grade). Ports tp.np.tr.ivecho.* tp.np.po_ar.u_multi.ordinal.sas.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: goodness_followup(): Goodness--follow-scatter plot showing actual vs. potential follow-per operation year. Ports tp.dp.gfup.R. goodness_event_plot(): Companion panel showing non-fatal event counts alongside goodness--follow-plot.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: trends_plot(): Annual trend lines optional confidence ribbons multiple groups. Ports tp.lp.trends.*, tp.rp.trends.*, tp.dp.trends.R template families. spaghetti_plot(): Individual patient trajectories time, optionally stratified group. Ports tp.dp.spaghetti.echo.R. longitudinal_counts_plot(): Grouped bar chart patient measurement counts discrete follow-windows. longitudinal_counts_table(): Numeric table panel counts, intended patchwork composition longitudinal_counts_plot().

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: alluvial_plot(): Alluvial (Sankey) diagram patient flow states across time points. Ports tp.dp.female_bicus_preAR_sankey.R. cluster_sankey_plot(): Cluster-stability Sankey showing patients redistribute across K values PAM analysis.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: eda_plot(): Single-variable EDA: bar chart (categorical) scatter + LOESS (continuous). Ports tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots.R. eda_classify_var(): Classify vector "Cont", "Cat_Num", "Cat_Char" using UniqueLimit heuristic Barplot_Scatterplot_Function.R. eda_select_vars(): Subset reorder data frame character vector space-separated column-name string. Replaces Order_Variables(). upset_plot(): UpSet intersection plot set membership across binary indicator columns. Ports tp.complexUpset.R.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: generator produces realistic synthetic data sized structured match corresponding SAS dataset exports. sample_mirror_histogram_data(): Propensity scores via logistic model greedy 1:1 caliper matching optional IPTW weights. sample_covariate_balance_data(): Standardised mean differences propensity matching. sample_stacked_histogram_data(): Year--category count data. sample_survival_data(): Exponential survival times administrative censoring optional treatment strata. sample_hazard_data(): Weibull parametric survival predictions fine time grid, matching predict dataset %hazpred. sample_hazard_empirical(): Binned Kaplan-Meier empirical points matching plout / acpdms dataset. sample_life_table(): Age-group-specific Gompertz survivorship curves matching US population life-table SAS overlays. sample_survival_difference_data(): Mean survival difference two parametric arms confidence bands. sample_nnt_data(): Number-needed--treat derived survival difference data. sample_nonparametric_curve_data(): Two-phase nonparametric curve predictions (binary continuous outcome) fine time grid. sample_nonparametric_curve_points(): Binned patient-level data summary points matching SAS means dataset. sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data(): Grade-specific cumulative proportional-odds probability curves. sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(): Binned ordinal data summary points per grade level. sample_goodness_followup_data(): Operative cohort operation dates, follow-times, death, non-fatal events. sample_trends_data(): Multi-group annual trend data confidence ribbons. sample_spaghetti_data(): Patient-level longitudinal measurements time. sample_longitudinal_counts_data(): Pre-aggregated patient measurement counts discrete follow-windows. sample_alluvial_data(): Patient flow states across multiple time points wide format. sample_cluster_sankey_data(): Cluster assignments across K values PAM stability analysis. sample_eda_data(): Mixed-type cardiac-surgery registry simulation (binary, ordinal, character-categorical, continuous variables). sample_upset_data(): Binary indicator columns UpSet intersection analysis.

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hvti_theme

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hvti_theme
text: style Character keyword identifying theme style. Supported values: "ppt" / "dark_ppt" — dark background, white text (hvti_theme_dark_ppt()); default PPT theme "light_ppt" — light/transparent background, black text (hvti_theme_light_ppt()) "manuscript" — clean white background journal figures (hvti_theme_manuscript()) "poster" — medium font conference posters (hvti_theme_poster()) ... Additional parameters forwarded underlying theme constructor.

text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hvti_theme
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) + geom_point()  p + hvti_theme("manuscript")   # journal figure  p + hvti_theme("poster")       # conference poster  p + hvti_theme("light_ppt")    # light-background slide   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Dark PPT — best viewed against a dark slide background p + hvti_theme("dark_ppt") +   ggplot2::theme(plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "navy")) } # }

text: Converts survfit object tidy data frame, prepends time=0 row per stratum, strips "s_=" prefix stratum names, computes derived columns match SAS %kaplan macro outputs:

title: Extract tidy data frame from a survfit object — km_extract_tidy

text: fit survfit object. group_col Name original stratification column, NULL.

title: Extract tidy data frame from a survfit object — km_extract_tidy
text: data frame.

title: Extract tidy data frame from a survfit object — km_extract_tidy
text: cumhaz Cumulative hazard H(t) = -log S(t). log_cumhaz log H(t) — y-axis log-log survival plot used assess proportional-hazards assumption. log_time log(t) — x-axis log-scale PLOTC plots. hazard Instantaneous hazard estimate: log(S(t_prev) / S(t)) / (t - t_prev).  defined event times delta_t > 0; NA censoring times. density Probability density: (S(t_prev) - S(t)) / delta_t. mid_time Midpoint interval (t_prev, t], used x-axis hazard-rate plots. life Cumulative integral survival function (restricted mean survival time) using SAS trapezoidal rule: LIFE += delta_t * (3*S(t) - S(t_prev)) / 2. proplife LIFE / t — proportionate life length.
